cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl:1:{"acceptance":"- [ ] JWT middleware installed in data-plane router as optional layer (runs when Authorization header present)\n- [ ] JwtIssuer constructed at startup in serve.rs (dev: random key with warning)\n- [ ] AuditAttribution populated on every audit write when ResolvedIdentity is present\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs migrated from x-axon-database to composite x-axon-tenant-database\n- [ ] gRPC service.rs gained optional JWT verify path\n- [ ] cutover_jwt_test.rs integration test with real JWT end-to-end\n- [ ] scripts/cutover-smoke.sh script runs end-to-end against dev server\n- [ ] cargo test --workspace green\n- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- [ ] Legacy auth paths (--no-auth, --guest-role, Tailscale) still work unchanged","closing_commit_sha":"1a378fe785612ab9a24241977cf60c0c13e150b9","created_at":"2026-04-15T11:54:30.489788638Z","description":"Finish wiring the ADR-018 auth stack into the real request pipeline. After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT middleware, thread AuditAttribution into audit writes (10-20 sites)\n- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — construct JwtIssuer at startup\n- crates/axon-server/src/service.rs — rename header constant, parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for removal\n- crates/axon-storage/* — no storage changes\n- crates/axon-core/* — no type changes\n\n## Verification\n\n- cargo test -p axon-server (all prior tests green + new cutover_jwt_test)\n- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh runs end-to-end against a dev This is the structural defect behind 33–142h hung workers.\n\n## Scope\n\n1. Add `Context context.Context` to `RunOptions` (or change signatures to `Run(ctx, opts)`). Default to `context.Background()` when nil.\n2. Derive the internal ctx from the caller's rather than `context.Background()`.\n3. Thread ctx through `agent.ExecuteBead` → `runner.Run` → `agentlib.Run` → provider HTTP.\n4. Update `server.WorkerManager.runWorker`'s `singleTierAttempt` closure to pass `ctx` into `agent.ExecuteBead`.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- New wall-clock timeout logic (covered by sibling RC2 bead)\n- Operator-facing stop command (covered by Regression anchor for ddx-0a651925 RC1: runner.Run and\\n+// Runner.RunAgent previously discarded the caller's ctx by constructing\\n+// their own context.WithCancel(context.Background()), so WorkerManager.Stop\\n+// could not cancel the in-flight agent call.\\n+func TestRunAgentCancelledContextReturnsPromptly(t *testing.T) {\\n+\\tprovider := \u0026sleepProvider{\\n+\\t\\tdelay: 30 * time.Second, // far larger than the 500ms AC deadline\\n+\\t\\tresponse: agentlib.Response{\\n+\\t\\t\\tContent: \\\"never-returned\\\",\\n+\\t\\t\\tModel:   \\\"test-model\\\",\\n+\\t\\t},\\n+\\t}\\n+\\n+\\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir()})\\n+\\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\\n+\\tr.AgentProvider = provider\\n+\\n+\\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+\\tcancel() // pre-cancel so the call returns on entry\\n+\\n+\\tstart := time.Now()\\n+\\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\\n+\\t\\tHarness: \\\"agent\\\",\\n+\\t\\tPrompt:  \\\"test\\\",\\n+\\t\\tContext: ctx,\\n+\\t})\\n+\\telapsed := time.Since(start)\\n+\\n+\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n+\\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\\n+\\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \\\"canceled run should report non-zero exit\\\")\\n+\\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \\\"canceled run should carry error text\\\")\\n+\\tassert.Less(t, elapsed, 500*time.Millisecond,\\n+\\t\\t\\\"canceled ctx must abort the provider call promptly; took %v\\\", elapsed)\\n+}\\n+\\n+// TestRunAgentContextCancelledMidRunReturnsPromptly cancels the caller's\\n+// context while the provider is blocked, then asserts that the runner\\n+// unwinds within 500ms of cancellation. This exercises the propagation\\n+// path (caller ctx → internal WithCancel(parent) → agentlib.Run → provider\\n+// Chat) rather than the short-circuit pre-canceled case.\\n+func TestRunAgentContextCancelledMidRunReturnsPromptly(t *testing.T) {\\n+\\tprovider := \u0026sleepProvider{\\n+\\t\\tdelay: 30 * time.Second,\\n+\\t\\tresponse: agentlib.Response{\\n+\\t\\t\\tContent: \\\"never-returned\\\",\\n+\\t\\t\\tModel:   \\\"test-model\\\",\\n+\\t\\t},\\n+\\t}\\n+\\n+\\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir()})\\n+\\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\\n+\\tr.AgentProvider = provider\\n+\\n+\\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+\\t// Cancel after a brief warmup so we measure the cancel→return latency.\\n+\\tgo func() {\\n+\\t\\ttime.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)\\n+\\t\\tcancel()\\n+\\t}()\\n+\\n+\\tstart := time.Now()\\n+\\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\\n+\\t\\tHarness: \\\"agent\\\",\\n+\\t\\tPrompt:  \\\"test\\\",\\n+\\t\\tContext: ctx,\\n+\\t})\\n+\\telapsed := time.Since(start)\\n+\\n+\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n+\\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\\n+\\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode)\\n+\\tassert.Less(t, elapsed, 500*time.Millisecond,\\n+\\t\\t\\\"caller cancel must propagate within 500ms; took %v\\\", elapsed)\\n+}\\n+\\n+// TestRunCancelledContextReturnsPromptlySubprocess verifies the same\\n+// cancellation contract for the subprocess dispatch path through Runner.Run.\\n+// The OSExecutor honours ctx by sending SIGKILL on ctx.Done, so a canceled\\n+// ctx must not leave the runner blocked on the child exit.\\n+func TestRunCancelledContextReturnsPromptlySubprocess(t *testing.T) {\\n+\\t// Use the mockExecutor (in-process fake) with a blocking delay. A\\n+\\t// canceled context should short-circuit execution before any exec\\n+\\t// happens, rather than waiting for the mock.\\n+\\texec := \u0026blockingMockExecutor{block: 30 * time.Second}\\n+\\tr := newTestRunner(\u0026mockExecutor{output: \\\"ok\\\"})\\n+\\tr.Executor = exec\\n+\\n+\\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\\n+\\tcancel()\\n+\\n+\\tstart := time.Now()\\n+\\tresult, err := r.Run(RunOptions{\\n+\\t\\tHarness: \\\"codex\\\",\\n+\\t\\tPrompt:  \\\"hello\\\",\\n+\\t\\tContext: ctx,\\n+\\t})\\n+\\telapsed := time.Since(start)\\n+\\n+\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n+\\trequire.NotNil(t, result)\\n+\\tassert.Less(t, elapsed, 500*time.Millisecond,\\n+\\t\\t\\\"canceled ctx must abort subprocess dispatch promptly; took %v\\\", elapsed)\\n+}\\n+\\n+// blockingMockExecutor is an Executor whose ExecuteInDir respects ctx: it\\n+// either blocks for `block` or returns ctx.Err() when ctx is canceled.\\n+type blockingMockExecutor struct {\\n+\\tblock time.Duration\\n+}\\n+\\n+func (e *blockingMockExecutor) Execute(ctx context.Context, binary string, args []string, stdin string) (*ExecResult, error) {\\n+\\treturn e.ExecuteInDir(ctx, binary, args, stdin, \\\"\\\")\\n+}\\n+\\n+func (e *blockingMockExecutor) ExecuteInDir(ctx context.Context, _ string, _ []string, _ string, _ string) (*ExecResult, error) {\\n+\\tselect {\\n+\\tcase \u003c-time.After(e.block):\\n+\\t\\treturn \u0026ExecResult{Stdout: \\\"\\\", ExitCode: 0}, nil\\n+\\tcase \u003c-ctx.Done():\\n+\\t\\treturn \u0026ExecResult{ExitCode: -1}, ctx.Err()\\n+\\t}\\n+}\\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/server/workers_stop_propagation_test.go b/cli/internal/server/workers_stop_propagation_test.go\\nnew file mode 100644\\nindex 00000000..4b7ce402\\n--- /dev/null\\n+++ b/cli/internal/server/workers_stop_propagation_test.go\\n@@ -0,0 +1,86 @@\\n+package server\\n+\\n+import (\\n+\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n+\\t\\\"sync/atomic\\\"\\n+\\t\\\"testing\\\"\\n+\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n+\\n+\\t\\\"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent\\\"\\n+\\t\\\"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\\\"\\n+\\t\\\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\\\"\\n+)\\n+\\n+// TestWorkerManagerStopCancelsAgentExecution is the regression test for\\n+// ddx-0a651925 RC1: WorkerManager.Stop(id) must cancel the in-flight\\n+// executor (and transitively the agent provider call) via ctx propagation,\\n+// not merely close the poll loop. the\\n+\\t// cancellation we exercise is against an in-flight call.\\n+\\trequire.Eventually(t, executorStarted.Load, 3*time.Second, 20*time.Millisecond,\\n+\\t\\t\\\"executor never entered the That path is why bundle `20260415T015231-c2ffa9ba` fired at exactly 2h 0m 0.098s — and nothing else does.\n\n## Scope\n\n1. Add an absolute wall-clock deadline in addition to (not replacing) the idle timeout.\n2. Default the wall-clock bound to something operators can trust — e.g. 3x `DefaultTimeoutMS` or a new `DefaultWallClockMS`.\n3. Apply in: `OSExecutor.ExecuteInDir` (subprocess path) and `Runner.RunAgent` (embedded agent path).\n4. Reflect the wall-clock error in result.json as a distinct `error: \"wall-clock deadline exceeded after Xh\"` so it is not confused with idle timeout.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- Lowering or removing the existing idle timeout.\n- Per-provider tuning (can be follow-up).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-18T05:29:50.370737575Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent .ddx/beads.jsonl:35:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.\n\n2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.\n\n3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.\n\n4. **First-pass perf targets.**\n   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.\n\n5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**\n   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.\n\n6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.\n\n7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.\n\n8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.\n\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.","claimed-at":"2026-04-23T01:08:27Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2988370","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:04.443214848Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThe Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be \"an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model\" — which is not what the current implementation does.\n\n## Ground truth (current implementation)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —\n\n- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.\n- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.\n- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.\n- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.\n\n## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)\n\n- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: \"how is harness/provider/model X doing?\" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.\n- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).\n- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Reshape the data source\n\n- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.\n- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.\n- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.\n- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.\n\n### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)\n\n- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.\n- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.\n- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.\n\n### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)\n\n- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.\n\n### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path\n\n- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.\n- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- The `comparisons` / \"compare arms\" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).\n- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).\n- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:51.254212382Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. Available models: Qwen3.5-122B-A10B-RAM-100GB-MLX, MiniMax-M2.5-MLX-4bit, Qwen3-Coder-Next-MLX-4bit, gemma-4-31B-it-MLX-4bit, Qwen3.5-27B-4bit, Qwen3.5-27B-Claude-4.6-Opus-Distilled-MLX-4bit, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit, Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-nvfp4, gpt-oss-20b-MXFP4-Q8\",\"type\":\"not_found_error\",\"param\":null,\"code\":null}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:51.450641001Z","kind":"tier-attempt","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"execution_failed"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_model\":\"codex/gpt-5.4\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:54.037179949Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=codex/gpt-5.4"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=standard harness=claude model=codex/gpt-5.4 probe=ok\nunsupported model \"codex/gpt-5.4\" for harness \"claude\"; .ddx/beads.jsonl:62:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer running `go test`, my test runs do not leave behind registered projects in my real `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`. When a machine has already been polluted, I can prune it with one command. The server sweep is aggressive enough that obvious test-dir pollution does not survive a restart.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Fix A — test isolation enforced by CI.**\n   - Every existing test that instantiates `ServerState` or boots a server helper is migrated to an isolated state dir. No test writes to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`.\n   - A lint test (Go or shell) parses `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` (and any other directories that wire up `ServerState`) and fails if it finds a construction that does not pass an explicit isolated state path or override `XDG_DATA_HOME` first. Test cases added for both the allowed-pattern and the disallowed-pattern.\n\n2. **Fix B — sweep recognizes test-dir pollution.**\n   - `ServerState.migrate` drops entries whose `Path` matches any of:\n     - prefix `/tmp/`, `/private/tmp/`, `/var/folders/`\n     - regex `/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\\d+/` anywhere in the path\n   - Missing paths matching these patterns are dropped on first sweep (no 24h tombstone).\n   - Reachable paths matching these patterns are still dropped (they are never real projects — the test dir may exist transiently but is never the user's registered project).\n   - Unit test seeds a fixture state with one real project, one `/tmp/TestFoo123/001` missing, one `/tmp/TestBar456/002` still on disk, and one `/private/tmp/TestMac789/001`; asserts only the real project survives.\n\n3. **Fix C — pruning CLI.**\n   - `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state \u003cpath\u003e]` applies the Fix B rules to an on-disk state file.\n   - Writes `server-state.json.bak-\u003cYYYYMMDDTHHMMSS\u003e` before overwriting.\n   - Prints summary: `Pruned X of Y entries (Z kept)`. Dry-run prints the summary without writing.\n   - Integration test uses a fixture state containing 10 real + 100 fake entries, runs the command, asserts 10 remain and a backup file with exactly 110 entries was written.\n\n4. **Perf amplifier — regression test.**\n   - A test asserts `GetBeadSnapshots` does **not** call `os.Stat` on any project already tombstoned or pattern-filtered. (Implementation detail: sweep on startup + skip tombstoned in the hot loop.)\n   - If Fix A+B are in place, a fresh `go test ./...` run followed by a server restart produces a state file with ≤ 20 projects on a typical dev machine. Asserted via a CI job that runs the tests, starts the server briefly, inspects the state file, and fails if test-dir patterns are present.\n\n5. **Backward compatibility.**\n   - The prune CLI is safe to run on an existing polluted state file. The sweep upgrade is safe on restart — no data loss for real projects.\n   - On upgrade, a note is logged: \"Pruned X phantom test-dir projects from state file.\"\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Bead notes link to `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) as the two surfaces whose perf problems are amplified by this bug. Those beads' fixture sizing assumptions were correct for a clean state file; the operator experience will still feel slow on a polluted machine until Fix C is run.","claimed-at":"2026-04-23T01:06:16Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2988370","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:44:13.806406362Z","description":"## Observed\n\n`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:\n\n- **6,936 named \"001\"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.\n- **344 named \"002\"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.\n- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).\n- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are \"alive\" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.\n- State file size: 1.8 MB.\n\n## Root cause\n\n1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.\n2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.\n3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.\n\n## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)\n\nBoth `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nThree independently-landable fixes:\n\n### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)\n\n- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).\n- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.\n- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.\n\n### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)\n\n- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.\n- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\\w+\\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.\n- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.\n\n### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI\n\n- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-\u003ctimestamp\u003e`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).\n- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.\n- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Moving state to SQLite.\n- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)\n- Renaming \"001\" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:39.952429066Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. .ddx/beads.jsonl:78:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I want a single view that shows every place my agents can run — endpoint providers and subprocess harnesses alike — with status, recent token usage, and quota utilization. When I click into one, I see a 7-day trend so I can tell if I'm about to run out.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Deliverable 1 — Unified view.**\n   - New `harnessStatuses` GraphQL resolver returns subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, gemini, agent) with: name, kind=`harness`, reachable, detail, lastCheckedAt, defaultForProfile.\n   - Existing `providerStatuses` shape gains `kind=\\\"endpoint\\\"` and `lastCheckedAt`.\n   - UI page loads within 500ms on a warm cache — it renders from last-known status **first**, then patches rows live as async probes return. Playwright asserts the table is interactive within 500ms of navigation and that row status patches within 5s of the probe completing.\n   - Subprocess harnesses claude and codex **appear in the table** on a machine where their binaries are installed, with correct reachable status. Integration test.\n\n2. **Deliverable 2 — Usage + quota fields (stubbable).**\n   - Per-row fields `usage` and `quota` as described. All fields nullable.\n   - For endpoint providers, `usage.tokensUsedLast24h` and `usage.tokensUsedLastHour` are computed from the sessions index (fixture: ≥1k session rows covering the last 24h). Unit test asserts correctness on a seeded window.\n   - For subprocess harnesses that expose rate-limit headers (claude, codex), parsed headers populate `quota.ceilingTokens`, `quota.ceilingWindowSeconds`, `quota.remaining`, `quota.resetAt`. Parser tested with captured header fixtures from each harness.\n   - For harnesses/providers that expose nothing, fields remain null and the UI renders `—` / \"not reported\"; no fabricated data.\n   - Utilization bar renders when both `usage` and `quota.ceilingTokens` are present. Sparkline renders when ≥6 hourly buckets of usage are available.\n\n3. **Deliverable 3 — Trend detail.**\n   - Detail route `/nodes/.../providers/[name]` shows 7-day and 30-day tokens/hour + requests/hour time series, with quota ceiling overlay when known.\n   - Buckets computed server-side from the sessions index; the client receives ≤ 200 data points per series.\n   - \"Projected to hit quota in ~X\" callout uses last-24h slope only. Renders only when `quota.ceilingTokens` is known and the last-24h slope is non-zero positive; otherwise absent.\n   - Playwright asserts the detail route renders with seeded usage and the projection text appears for a fixture that has a ceiling + ascending usage.\n\n4. **Perf posture.**\n   - Fixture for perf test: ≥1,000 session index rows covering the last 30 days with usage distributed across ≥5 endpoints/harnesses. Per standing directive in `ddx-9ce6842a`.\n   - Targets: unified list p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP (excluding async probes); trend detail p95 ≤ 400ms HTTP on the fixture.\n   - If in-place aggregation cannot hit the detail-view p95 on a 10,000-row stretch fixture, pause and raise the DB-substrate decision with the user per the standing directive — do not silently switch.\n\n5. **Design note.**\n   - One-pager in `docs/helix/02-design/` before implementation recording the page-rename decision, the row model (common fields, kind-specific fields), async-probe strategy, and the quota source per harness/provider. PR links to it.\n\n6. **No regressions.**\n   - `ddx agent providers`, `ddx agent doctor`, `ddx agent list`, `ddx agent check` CLI commands continue to work unchanged.\n   - `defaultRouteStatus` query remains.\n\n7. **Cross-references.**\n   - Depends on `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) for usage aggregation. If that bead is not yet landed when this work starts, ship Deliverable 1 standalone with usage/quota stubbed as \"not available,\" and re-open the remaining deliverables when the index is ready.","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:48:37.871527387Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThree related issues on `/nodes/.../providers`:\n\n1. **Loads slowly.** The page fires two GraphQL queries on mount — `providerStatuses` (which probes every configured provider for live connectivity) and `defaultRouteStatus` (which resolves the default route). Both are synchronous and serialized only by `Promise.all`. Each provider probe is a real network call.\n2. **Missing harnesses.** The pane shows API-endpoint providers (qwen, minimax, etc. — anything wired through the service with an endpoint). It does not show subprocess-invoked harnesses like `claude` (Claude Code) or `codex`. That is accurate to the current `providerStatuses` resolver (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_providers.go:15-50`, which calls `svc.ListProviders`) but not what an operator wants: the operator thinks of \"where their agent work can run,\" which includes both endpoint providers AND subprocess harnesses.\n3. **No quota visibility.** Operator need: see current token utilization rate + quota utilization per harness/provider, trend it, and use the trend to decide whether to throttle before hitting a cap.\n\n## Root cause by area\n\n- **Slow load:** `svc.ListProviders` probes every configured provider for liveness. Probes are serial within the service call, and the whole query blocks page render.\n- **Missing harnesses:** Subprocess harnesses (claude, codex, gemini) are first-class in the CLI (`ddx agent list`, `ddx agent doctor`, `ddx agent check`) but are not surfaced via `providerStatuses`. They have their own status concepts — binary installed, credentials configured, rate-limit headroom — that don't map cleanly onto the endpoint-provider model.\n- **No quota:** No existing data source captures rolling token usage vs quota. Quota information varies wildly by harness: Claude Code has message / session headroom concepts from response headers; codex has rate-limit headers; endpoint providers have API quota per key. There is no unified representation today.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nThree deliverables, sequenced from easy to hard.\n\n### Deliverable 1 — Unified \"Agent endpoints\" view (renames/rescopes the page)\n\n- Rename the surface from \"Providers\" to **\"Agent endpoints\"** (or similar — confirm copy in the design note). The domain object is \"a place where agent work can run,\" not \"an API provider.\"\n- Merge the data: `providerStatuses` stays as the source for endpoint providers. A new `harnessStatuses` resolver (read from `svc.ListHarnesses` / existing `ddx agent list` + `ddx agent doctor` machinery) surfaces subprocess harnesses. Page renders both in one table with a `kind` column (`endpoint` | `harness`) — or two tables if design prefers.\n- Status fields common to both: name, kind, reachable (bool), detail (text), last-checked-at, default-for-profile (set of profile names).\n- Asynchronous liveness. The page renders an instant first paint from cached status; liveness probes fire in background and patch rows as they return. No synchronous probe-wall on load.\n\n### Deliverable 2 — Token \u0026 quota fields\n\n- Extend the status payload (per row) with: `usage { tokensUsedLastHour, tokensUsedLast24h, requestsLastHour, requestsLast24h }` and `quota { ceilingTokens?, ceilingWindowSeconds?, remaining?, resetAt? }`. Any field may be null if the provider/harness doesn't expose it.\n- Data sources:\n  - **Endpoint providers:** aggregate from the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) filtered to the last 1h / 24h, grouped by provider. Quota ceilings come from any headers we capture during calls, plus optional static config.\n  - **Subprocess harnesses:** same sessions-index aggregation for usage. Quota — parse rate-limit headers from the harness output where available (Claude Code, codex both emit them). For harnesses that don't, leave quota null and render \"not reported.\"\n- Row gains two small inline displays: utilization bar (used / ceiling for the quota window) and a 7-day sparkline of tokens/hour.\n\n### Deliverable 3 — Trend view\n\n- Click into a row → detail page showing: 7-day and 30-day time-series of tokens/hour and requests/hour, with the quota ceiling overlaid. Aggregations come from the sessions index, bucketed server-side to avoid shipping raw rows.\n- Simple \"projected to hit quota in ~X hours/days\" callout computed from the slope of the last 24h — enough to answer \"are we on a trend to run out?\" without building a forecasting system.\n\n## Cross-bead dependencies\n\n- Depends on `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) for any usage-rate data. If that bead isn't landed when this work starts, the usage/quota features stub to \"not available\" and ship purely with the unified-view rename + async liveness.\n- Fixture / perf guidance: per the standing directive recorded in `ddx-9ce6842a`, any aggregation here is tested against ≥1k session records. If in-place aggregation can't hit perf targets, bring the DB-substrate question to the user before picking one.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:01:01.045566953Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. .ddx/beads.jsonl:83:{"claimed-at":"2026-04-15T01:13:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:11:17.497753676Z","description":"The embedded 'agent' harness can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.\n\n## Observed\n\nRunning 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --harness agent --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):\n\n1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.\n\n2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:\n\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    ...\n\n3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.\n\n4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same harness/model and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.\n\n## Expected\n\n- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate\n- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after \u003c messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely\n- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin\n\n## Scope\n\n- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status\n- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn\n- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls\n- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\n- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master\n- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally\n- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic\n- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome\n- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"merged onto current tip\nresult_rev=604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0\nbase_rev=9943221220ec53cace570413e07dc6e809a5995a","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:39:52.60471501Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"success"},{"actor":"audit-reviewer","body":"The DDx-side circuit breaker in commit 604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0 is a caller-layer workaround. The root cause (compaction driver producing 8461+ no-op events without a native bound) belongs upstream in github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent.\n\nFiled upstream bead agent-e5228424 in /home/erik/Projects/agent/.ddx/beads.jsonl with:\n- The bug description\n- Pointer to DDx workaround commit as defense-in-depth\n- Requested native fix (bounded compaction attempts + structured ErrStuck)\n- Regression test requirement (seeded stuck-state reproduction)\n\nWhen the upstream fix lands and DDx bumps the agent library version, the caller-side circuit breaker can be removed.","created_at":"2026-04-15T03:46:00.187180985Z","kind":"note","source":"upstream companion bead filed 2026-04-15","summary":"Upstream native fix requested at github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent: bead agent-e5228424 (compaction loop native stuck detection). DDx circuit breaker is defense-in-depth; can be removed once upstream fix lands."}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T01:39:31.55993889Z","id":"ddx-25a41f51","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"],"owner":"ddx","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-e80e780a","status":"closed","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","updated_at":"2026-04-15T03:46:00.206978601Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:99:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I expect the Sessions page to reflect reality — if I ran `ddx work` today, today's sessions appear within seconds. And I expect the feed not to silently break again the next time the agent execution path is refactored.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Session-index source of truth.** A single writer (invoked from `service.Execute` or a thin wrapper that every production agent-execution path calls) appends one line per completed agent invocation to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`. Fields are pointer-only: id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath (relative), nativeLogRef (relative). No prompt or response bodies embedded.\n\n2. **Single-call-site guarantee.** Unit test enumerates every production code path that executes an agent (execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, replay if still present) and asserts each appends exactly one index row. If a new path is added later without wiring this up, the test fails.\n\n3. **Visibility restored.** GraphQL `sessions` query reads `sessions.index.jsonl`. Running `ddx work` (or any agent-execution path) against a fixture project causes the new session to appear in a GraphQL query within 2 seconds of completion. E2E test demonstrates this.\n\n4. **Body lazy-load.** A new resolver `session(id: ID!) { prompt response stderr }` loads the heavy fields on demand from the execution bundle (`.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/`) or the agent-log path, without reading every session in the index.\n\n5. **Legacy migration.** A one-shot command (e.g. `ddx agent log reindex`) seeds the index from the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (397 MB corpus on the current repo), preserving historical sessions. Integration test uses a fixture `sessions.jsonl` and asserts the index has one row per legacy entry with all pointer fields populated.\n\n6. **Deprecated-file behavior.** After migration, the legacy `sessions.jsonl` is not read by the server. `ddx agent usage` and related CLI commands (`cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:197,246,348`, `cli/cmd/status.go:179`) are updated to read from the index too, verified by their existing tests.\n\n7. **Perf sanity.** The GraphQL `sessions` list resolves in ≤100ms p95 on a fixture with 10,000 index entries; current behavior times out or OOMs. Benchmark captured in bead notes as baseline + after-fix.\n\n8. **UI note.** If there is a gap in the index (e.g. the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap on the current repo, assuming backfill from executions/agent-logs is not feasible), the Sessions page surfaces a single-line banner \"No sessions recorded between X and Y\" so the gap is not silently invisible. If backfill is feasible, do it instead and skip the banner.\n\n9. **Playwright e2e.**\n   - Seeds a fixture project with `.ddx/executions/` bundles from both before and after a simulated gap.\n   - Runs the migration command.\n   - Opens `/sessions`, asserts the most recent session timestamp matches the latest bundle, asserts a clicked row lazy-loads prompt/response from the bundle rather than from an embedded payload.\n   - Asserts the feed updates live (within 2s) after a new simulated session is written.","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:22:41.990684692Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThe Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.\n\n## Root cause\n\nThe Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.\n\nThe only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`\n- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`\n- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`\n\nProduction execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.\n\n## Secondary issue (same fix should address)\n\n`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nDo not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:\n\n1. **Decide the source of truth for \"a session\".** Current candidates on disk:\n   - `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.\n   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.\n   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).\n   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.\n\n2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.\n\n3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.\n\n4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.\n\n5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.\n- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:17.183631171Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","feature":"FEAT-010","id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-006","perf","observability"],"notes":"## 2026-04-22 — User-directed amendment: shard the index\n\nPer user feedback: a single `sessions.index.jsonl` just replays the same problem at smaller scale. The aggregate needs to be split.\n\n### Revised storage shape\n\n- Directory: `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/` (per-project, under each registered project's `.ddx`).\n- Inside: **monthly shards** named `sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`.\n- Each shard is pointer-only, same fields as the previously-proposed index (id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath, nativeLogRef).\n- Writer resolves the target shard from `time.Now().UTC()` at append time and creates the shard file lazily on first write of the month.\n- Heavy bodies (prompt, response, stderr) stay where they already live — `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` and agent-log streams — and are lazy-loaded by the `session(id:)` resolver.\n\n### Why monthly, not daily / size-based / SQLite\n\n- **Daily** — produces tens of thousands of files over the project lifetime; ReadDir becomes the cost.\n- **Size-based** — requires coordinating rotation with the append; race-prone and hard to test.\n- **SQLite** — overkill for first pass; revisit if a single monthly shard grows past tens of MB.\n- **Monthly** — ≤12 shards/year, human-readable filenames, date-range queries map trivially to shard selection, bounded shard size in practice.\n\n### Reader behavior\n\n- Default Sessions query loads the **current + previous shard** (covers \"recent activity\" for any current date).\n- Date-range queries map the range to a shard set and read only those.\n- Pagination beyond the default window triggers older-shard loads on demand.\n- No shard-coordination lock needed: each month's file is append-only and only the current-month shard is actively written.\n\n### Legacy migration\n\n- `ddx agent log reindex` reads the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` once and splits by each entry's `timestamp` field into the appropriate monthly shards under `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/`.\n- After migration, the legacy file is renamed `sessions.jsonl.legacy` (not deleted) and never read again by the server. Hand-removal is the user's call.\n- Idempotent: re-running the command merges without duplicating (index row dedup by `id`).\n\n### Revised acceptance criteria (supersedes criteria 1, 5, 6, 7 on original description)\n\n1. **Sharded index writer.** Single shared call site (from `service.Execute` or equivalent wrapper) appends one line to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, computing the shard from `time.Now().UTC()`. Test: write across a simulated month boundary and assert two shards exist with the correct split.\n\n5. **Legacy migration shards too.** `ddx agent log reindex` splits `sessions.jsonl` into monthly shards by entry timestamp. Integration test uses a fixture with entries across 3 distinct months and asserts 3 shards are produced with correct contents and no duplicates on re-run.\n\n6. **Server reads the shard directory.** `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` reads `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/*.jsonl`, respects any date-range arg, and loads only the shards that intersect the range. Unit test seeds 6 monthly shards and asserts a 2-month query reads exactly 2 files.\n\n7. **Perf on sharded index.** Default Sessions view (current + previous shard) resolves in ≤50ms p95 on a fixture with 12 monthly shards × 10k entries each (120k total). Capture baseline (single-file read of same corpus) vs sharded in bead notes.\n\n(Criteria 2, 3, 4, 8, 9 from the original acceptance list remain as written.)\n\n### Scope clarifier\n\nThis amendment does not change the single-call-site writer guarantee or the lazy-body resolver — those remain the load-bearing ideas. The amendment is specifically about **how the pointer index is physically stored**.\n## 2026-04-23 — Implementation benchmark and evidence\n\nBaseline: `go test ./internal/agent -run '^$' -bench 'Benchmark(ReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow|LegacySessionsJSONLFullScan)$' -benchtime=1x` measured legacy single-file full scan/sort of 120k legacy-shaped rows with 2KB embedded responses at `BenchmarkLegacySessionsJSONLFullScan-12 1 1731705957 ns/op`.\n\nAfter fix: the same command measured the sharded default-window reader at `BenchmarkReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow-12 1 8103661 ns/op` for 12 monthly shards x 10k rows each (120k total), with the default query reading only the current + previous shard window. Environment footer: linux/arm64, Go go1.26.2.\n\nAcceptance evidence: `TestSessionIndexWritesMonthlyShards`, `TestSessionIndexShardFilesSelectsOnlyIntersectingDateRange`, `TestReindexLegacySessionsSplitsShardsAndDedupes`, `TestRunViaServiceWithAppendsOneSessionIndexRow`, `TestProductionAgentExecutionPathsUseIndexedServiceWriter`, `TestGraphQLAgentSessions`, `TestAgentLogReindexMigratesLegacyFile`, existing agent usage/status/processmetrics tests, and `e2e/sessions.spec.ts` cover writer sharding, bounded reads, migration, lazy body loading, deprecated-file read removal, UI gap banner, and live refresh.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\nMissing session index writer: no changes to `service.Execute`, shared execution wrappers, or agent execution paths to append `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`.\nMissing GraphQL changes: no update to `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go` for index-backed `sessions` or lazy `session(id:)` body loading.\nMissing migration command: no `ddx agent log reindex` or equivalent CLI implementation and no fixture-based migration test.\nMissing CLI usage/status updates: no changes to `cli/cmd/agent_usage.go` or `cli/cmd/status.go` to stop reading legacy `sessions.jsonl`.\nMissing performance validation: no benchmark or fixture proving 10,000 index entries resolve within the required threshold.\nMissing UI gap/banner behavior and Playwright coverage for `/sessions`, lazy loading, migration, and live feed updates.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**AC 1–3**: No `sessions.index.jsonl` schema, writer in `service.Execute`, or updated GraphQL resolver in `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go`.\n**AC 4**: No `session(id: ID!)` resolver for lazy-loading prompt/response/stderr from execution bundles.\n**AC 5**: No `ddx agent log reindex` command or migration logic.\n**AC 6**: `cli/cmd/agent_usage.go` and `cli/cmd/status.go` are untouched.\n**AC 7**: No benchmark captured.\n**AC 8–9**: No frontend changes or Playwright tests.","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"Sessions UI is blind to post-2026-04-19 activity: service.Execute path no longer writes sessions.jsonl","updated_at":"2026-04-23T01:06:16.432692308Z"}

Verified by reading the updated implementations in `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:gitShow` and `cli/cmd/bead_review.go:beadReviewGitShow` — both invoke `git show \u003crev\u003e -- . ':(exclude)...'` (or functional equivalent) with the documented exclusion set.\n\n2. Regression test: a unit test builds a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt against a synthetic evidence commit whose diff adds a `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/embedded/agent-fake.jsonl` containing \u003e=20,000 `+`-prefixed lines. The test asserts the resulting prompt is below a fixed token budget (suggest 150k tokens, ~600k bytes) and does NOT contain any line matching `agent-fake.jsonl`.\n\n3. Token-budget guard: a unit test asserts that when a `\u003cbead-review\u003e` prompt would exceed 150k tokens even after the exclusion, the builder elides to a summary stub (per suggested fix #2) and logs a warning rather than emitting the full content.\n\n4. Execution-evidence staging exclusion: `landEvidence` (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go`) and `VerifyCleanWorktree` (`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:VerifyCleanWorktree`) stage the evidence directory with a pathspec that excludes `embedded/` (matching the existing `SynthesizeCommit` exclusion at `execute_bead.go:269-283`), OR move raw session logs out of the tracked evidence directory entirely (suggested fix #3). Covered by a regression test that runs a complete execute-bead attempt end-to-end in a temporary repo and asserts `git log -p --follow '.ddx/executions/*/embedded/*.jsonl'` returns nothing (no tracked session log).\n\n5. End-to-end reproduction of the `hx-dd4ab35f`-style scenario: an integration test simulates the nexiq sequence (attempt lands impl commit, evidence commit adds 20k-line session log, review-malfunction triggers retry) and asserts the retry's synthesized `prompt.md` is under the budget cap AND the review call succeeds against a stub provider without an n_keep/n_ctx error.\n\n6. Existing execution evidence continues to satisfy auditability: either still tracked but excluded from review diffs, or moved to a dedicated ref / ignored store per the chosen fix. `ddx agent metrics` and `ddx bead metrics` still surface per-attempt usage/tokens/cost from the evidence bundle after the fix.","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T00:13:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1551897","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:08:23.667089738Z","description":"## Summary\n\nOn retry, DDx synthesizes a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt whose \u003cdiff\u003e section is the output of `git show \u003cresultRev\u003e`. When the prior attempt's landing sequence appended an evidence commit of the form `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt_id\u003e]` that added `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt_id\u003e/embedded/agent-*.jsonl` (full per-event NDJSON session log) as a tracked file, `resultRev` is that evidence commit's SHA (because `landEvidence` sets `result.NewTip = sha` and the orchestrator copies `land.NewTip` into `res.ResultRev`). `git show` of the evidence commit emits the entire session log diff with a `+` prefix per line. Session logs are 5-50k lines; the retry prompt exceeds every public context window.\n\n## Category / severity / surface\n\n- Category: correctness / resource-exhaustion\n- Severity: high\n- Surface: `ddx agent execute-bead` retry path triggered by `kind:review-malfunction` (and any other flow that re-synthesizes a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt from a git diff/show since base_rev).\n\n## Full bug description (verbatim)\n\nOn retry, DDx synthesizes a \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt whose \u003cdiff\u003e section is the raw `git diff \u003cbase_rev\u003e..\u003cresult_rev\u003e` of the prior attempt. The prior attempt's landing sequence includes an evidence commit of the form:\n\n    chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt_id\u003e]\n\nthat adds `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt_id\u003e/embedded/agent-*.jsonl` — the full per-event NDJSON session log of the prior attempt — as a tracked file. Because that file is *added* in the diff range, the diff includes it in full with a `+` prefix on every line. Session logs are routinely 5-50k lines. One reasonable fix iteration is enough to push the retry prompt past 1-2 million tokens, which no currently supported model can accept — the provider streams back an n_keep \u003e n_ctx error and the retry crashes without a request round-trip.\n\nThis is a self-poisoning loop: the \"evidence\" DDx commits to make attempts auditable is the exact artifact that makes the next attempt undeliverable.\n\n## Reproduction (observed live 2026-04-17 in nexiq)\n\n1. Bead `hx-dd4ab35f` claimed by execute-loop worker (harness=agent, model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b, provider=vidar).\n2. Attempt `20260417T211600-d6a7dee6` succeeded and landed two commits: `cb5e733` (implementation + regression test [hx-dd4ab35f]) and `9a006dd` (chore: add execution evidence [20260417T211600-], adds `.ddx/executions/20260417T211600-d6a7dee6/embedded/agent-1776460560227794836.jsonl`, +20,623 lines).\n3. Post-merge review emitted `BLOCK` without a rationale; DDx recorded `kind:review-malfunction` and reopened the bead for retry.\n4. Retry `20260417T212928-27f9054a` synthesized `.ddx/executions/20260417T212928-27f9054a/prompt.md` of 6,307,997 bytes; `\u003cbead-review\u003e` block = 6,307,969 bytes; 99.9998% is the diff of the prior evidence commit, 99%+ of that diff is the session log file. Rest of \u003cbead-review\u003e (title/description/acceptance/labels/governing) is ~28 bytes.\n5. lmstudio responded: \"n_keep: 2,274,074 \u003e= n_ctx: 196,608\". Worker recorded failed attempt with zero usage. Same pattern on vidar (n_keep: 1,169,147) and bragi (n_keep: 2,274,074), all running qwen3.6-35b-a3b at max_context_length 262144. Provider/model is irrelevant — prompt exceeds every public context window.\n\n## DDx code-path verification (Claude investigation 2026-04-17)\n\nRelevant files and line ranges in /Users/erik/Projects/ddx:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:553-561` — `DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow(rev)` runs `git -C \u003cprojectRoot\u003e show \u003crev\u003e` with no pathspec. Output is injected raw into `\u003cdiff\u003e` by `BuildReviewPrompt` at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:383-384`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:427-431` — reviewer fetches `diff := r.gitShow(resultRev)` then builds the prompt.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:540-555` (`landEvidence`) — creates `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cshort\u003e]` and sets `result.NewTip = sha`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:428-434` — `RealLandingGitOps.StageDir` runs `git add -- \u003crelPath\u003e` on the entire `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/` directory with no exclusions for `embedded/`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:250-251` — after Land, `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`, so `ResultRev` ends up pointing at the evidence commit. The reviewer then runs `git show \u003cevidence-commit\u003e`, which is literally the dump of the session log.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1135-1164` (`VerifyCleanWorktree`) — also stages the evidence dir via `git add -- \u003cevidenceDir\u003e` and commits `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cshort\u003e]`; same no-exclusion pattern.\n- `cli/cmd/bead_review.go:162-171` (`beadReviewGitShow`) — mirror of the same bug in the standalone `ddx bead review` command.\n\nConfirmation the bug reporter's claim about `:(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded`:\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:269-283` (`SynthesizeCommit`) — the exclusion pathspec IS present here; the bug is that the evidence-landing commit path (`StageDir` + `VerifyCleanWorktree`) and the review path (`git show \u003crev\u003e`) do not share this exclusion.\n- `cli/cmd/init.go:19-46` — the gitignore rules DO include `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/`, but `git add -- \u003cdir\u003e` on pre-existing or explicitly-added paths can still stage, and older workspaces may pre-date this gitignore (matches nexiq's scenario where the evidence commit landed the session log).\n\n## Deviation from bug description (minor)\n\nThe bug text describes the diff as `git diff \u003cbase_rev\u003e..\u003cresult_rev\u003e`. The code actually does `git show \u003cresultRev\u003e` (single-rev show). The self-poisoning mechanism is functionally identical: when `resultRev` points at the evidence commit (`landEvidence` + `res.ResultRev = land.NewTip`), `git show` emits the entire `+`-prefixed session log. All suggested fixes still apply.\n\n## Suggested fixes (ascending scope)\n\n1. Exclude execution-evidence paths from bead-review diffs by passing a pathspec to the `git show`/`git diff` call in the prompt builder: `-- . ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/embedded/*' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/prompt.md' ':(exclude).ddx/executions/*/usage.json'`. The same exclusion already exists at `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:269-283` for `SynthesizeCommit`; replicate it in `DefaultBeadReviewer.gitShow` (`execute_bead_review.go:553-561`), `beadReviewGitShow` (`cli/cmd/bead_review.go:162-171`), `landEvidence` → `StageDir` (`execute_bead_land.go:428-434,540-555`), and `VerifyCleanWorktree` (`execute_bead.go:1135-1164`).\n2. Substitute, don't reproduce. When a tracked-file addition exceeds a byte/line budget in the diff, replace it with a one-line stub such as: `+++ b/.ddx/executions/.../agent-*.jsonl  (+20,623 lines of session evidence — omitted from review prompt)`.\n3. Stop committing raw session logs as tracked evidence. Move `.ddx/executions/*/embedded/*.jsonl` to a gitignored store, or a separate `refs/ddx/evidence/*` ref analogous to `refs/ddx/iterations/*`.\n4. Cap the \u003cbead-review\u003e prompt size with a hard budget (e.g., 150k tokens). Elide to summary + log warning when exceeded.\n\n(1) is the minimal surgical fix; (3) is the correct long-term fix.\n\n## Evidence in nexiq repo\n\n- Bloated retry prompt: `.ddx/executions/20260417T212928-27f9054a/prompt.md` (6,307,997 bytes; \u003cbead-review\u003e body = 6,307,969 bytes).\n- Successful prior attempt: `.ddx/executions/20260417T211600-d6a7dee6/` (landed cb5e733 + 9a006dd).\n- Failing agent session: `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-1776460487823541600.jsonl` (session.end payload has the n_keep/n_ctx error verbatim).\n- Bead event trail: `ddx bead show hx-dd4ab35f` — routing, then `kind:review-malfunction summary:BLOCK without rationale`, then `kind:execute-bead summary:review_malfunction`.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T00:13:24.428420423Z","id":"ddx-39e27896","issue_type":"bug","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:bug","area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:prompt-synthesis"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"execute-bead retry prompt explodes when prior attempt committed session-log evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-18T00:29:15.347462806Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:219:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer looking at the Sessions page, I can tell at a glance how much I actually paid in cash versus what I consumed under subscriptions versus what I ran locally. No number on the page conflates those three categories.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **`billingMode` field.** Session records (index rows + live writer) carry `billingMode` ∈ `{paid, subscription, local}`. Enum is validated on write. Unit test covers each value.\n\n2. **Classifier.** `billingModeFor(harness, surface, baseURL)` is the single source of truth. Test table includes at minimum:\n   - claude (Claude Code) → subscription\n   - codex → subscription\n   - openrouter (any model) → paid\n   - openai/anthropic with API key → paid\n   - agent (embedded) → local\n   - openai-compat endpoint pointing at localhost/127.*/192.168.*/10.* → local\n   - openai-compat endpoint pointing at a public host with an API key → paid\n\n3. **Reindex backfill.** Running `ddx agent log reindex` (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) on a fixture with sessions from all three modes produces index rows with correct `billingMode`. No row is written without one.\n\n4. **Rollup resolver.** GraphQL exposes `sessionsCostSummary(projectId, since?, until?) { cashUsd, subscriptionEquivUsd, localSessionCount, localEstimatedUsd }`. Returns zeros/null correctly when no sessions match. Respects shard-scoped reads from `ddx-0a33bc5f`.\n\n5. **UI — three cards.** The Sessions page replaces the \"Total Cost\" card with three cards as described (Cash paid, Subscription value, Local sessions). Tooltips explain each bucket. Playwright asserts all three render and that hover on each shows the explanatory text.\n\n6. **UI — per-row badge.** Each session row's cost column shows a small `cash`/`sub`/`local` badge with a tooltip explaining the bucket. Playwright asserts badge presence and correct classification on a seeded fixture containing one session of each type.\n\n7. **Zero-state.** When the project has zero sessions of a bucket, that card shows `—` / `0`, not `$0.00`, to avoid implying a paid-zero vs absent-data collision. Playwright asserts.\n\n8. **Optional local cost config.** Setting `cost.local_per_1k_tokens` in `.ddx/config.yaml` causes the Local card to render as dollars using the configured rate × sum(totalTokens) for local sessions. Unset: count-only. Integration test covers both paths.\n\n9. **Accessibility.** The three cards and per-row badges expose their bucket meaning via `aria-label`, not just tooltip, so screen-reader users get the same clarification.\n\n10. **Cross-references.**\n    - Depends on `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) — the `billingMode` field must land in the index schema.\n    - Bead notes in `ddx-23978824` should reference this bead since the unified-endpoints view has overlapping concepts (what's a paid endpoint vs a subscription harness vs a local endpoint).","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:54:25.174982037Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThe Sessions page ($ sums `session.cost` into one \"Total Cost\" card, `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/.../sessions/+page.svelte:21,28,91`). The underlying `cost_usd` field is fed from three very different billing realities and the UI treats them as one:\n\n1. **Cash paid (pay-per-token).** API providers (OpenRouter, direct OpenAI, direct Anthropic API). `cost_usd` = actual billed amount.\n2. **Subscription-equivalent.** Claude Code, Codex — flat-rate subscriptions. The CLI/harness emits `total_cost_usd` as the **dollar-equivalent** for tokens consumed under the subscription (what you would have paid pay-per-token). You paid a flat fee up front; this is a value-extracted number, not a cash-out number.\n3. **Local compute.** Embedded `agent` harness, ollama/vllm endpoints. Today emits cost 0 because no one is billing; real cost is electricity + hardware amortization, not currently modeled.\n\nA user reading \"Total Cost: $X\" does not know which of those X is.\n\n## What's needed\n\nThree independent rollups, each explicitly labeled, each computable without ambiguity:\n\n1. **Cash** — sum of `cost_usd` across sessions whose billing mode is `paid`.\n2. **Subscription-equivalent** — sum across sessions whose billing mode is `subscription`. Copy: \"Dollar-equivalent for tokens consumed via Claude Code / Codex subscription. You did not pay this in cash; it's the pay-per-token rate you would have been charged.\"\n3. **Local** — either a count of sessions (if no rate is configured) or a computed dollar estimate if `local_cost_per_1k_tokens` is set in config. Default: count-only, to avoid inventing numbers.\n\n## Backing data model\n\nSession records (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) need a new field `billingMode: \"paid\" | \"subscription\" | \"local\"`. Derive at write time:\n\n- `paid`: harness is an API-endpoint provider (openrouter, openai-compat with an API key, anthropic API, …). Probably any harness with `Surface == \"openai-compat\"` or a known public-api provider prefix.\n- `subscription`: harness is `claude` (Claude Code) or `codex` or `gemini-cli` when using a Google account. Identify by harness name + surface.\n- `local`: harness is `agent` (embedded) OR endpoint is localhost / private IP / configured-as-local in provider config.\n\nClassification lives in one place — a small `billingModeFor(harness, surface, baseURL)` function — so the logic isn't duplicated between writer and reader.\n\n## Scope\n\n### Part 1 — Data model\n\n- Extend `SessionEntry` / the session index row with `billingMode string` (enum validated on write).\n- Add `billingModeFor(...)` helper + unit tests for each harness/surface combination.\n- Back-fill: `ddx agent log reindex` (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) computes `billingMode` when re-shard-writing legacy sessions.\n\n### Part 2 — Backend rollup\n\n- Modify the sessions GraphQL query (or add a sibling `sessionsCostSummary(projectId, since?)`) to return `{ cashUsd, subscriptionEquivUsd, localSessionCount, localEstimatedUsd? }`.\n- Reuse the perf-safe path from `ddx-0a33bc5f` (sessions-index aggregation, shard-aware date filter).\n\n### Part 3 — UI\n\n- Replace the single \"Total Cost\" card on the Sessions page with three cards:\n  - **Cash paid** ($X.XX) — tooltip \"Billed by pay-per-token APIs (OpenRouter, direct API keys)\"\n  - **Subscription value** ($X.XX) — tooltip \"Dollar-equivalent for tokens consumed under Claude Code / Codex subscriptions. Not cash out of pocket.\"\n  - **Local sessions** (N) — tooltip \"Sessions served locally. Compute cost not modeled.\" If `local_cost_per_1k_tokens` is set, show \"$X.XX est.\" instead of a count.\n- Per-row: small badge `cash` / `sub` / `local` in the cost column, color-coded (green/blue/gray). Tooltip explains the row's bucket.\n\n### Part 4 — Optional config\n\n- `.ddx/config.yaml` gains `cost.local_per_1k_tokens: \u003cfloat\u003e`. When set, local-session total gets rendered as dollars. When unset, count-only. No auto-estimation.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Subscription flat-rate cost modeling (\"I pay $20/month for Claude Code\"). A useful adjacent bead: divide flat rate by consumption to show $/1M-tokens realized value. Not here.\n- Quota trend / headroom. Covered by `ddx-23978824`.\n- Reclassifying historical sessions beyond what reindex can infer from their stored harness/surface fields.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:01:11.976218807Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found.
