.ddx/beads.jsonl:221:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator looking at the DDx web UI, I want to understand at a glance what Workers are vs Sessions, and I want to stop a misbehaving worker or start a new queue drain without dropping to a terminal, so the UI is a real operator surface instead of a read-only status page.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Copy + IA.** Workers page header includes a one-sentence description distinguishing it from Sessions. Sessions page likewise. Each links to the other. Playwright asserts both descriptions are present and the cross-links navigate.\n\n2. **Worker → Sessions navigation.** On worker detail, a \"Sessions\" section lists sessions produced by that worker (join on `session.workerId`). Empty state reads \"No sessions recorded yet.\" If session records do not yet carry a worker ID, that gap is filled as part of this bead and backfilled for existing sessions in the current run (no historical backfill required).\n\n3. **Session → Worker backlink.** Expanded session row shows worker ID as a clickable link when present.\n\n4. **Stop worker.** Each row with `state == 'running'` shows a Stop button. Clicking prompts for confirm, then fires a GraphQL mutation that stops the worker. UI updates within 2s via the existing subscription/poll path. Integration test asserts: button visibility is gated on state, confirm dialog is required, and mutation invokes the same code path as `ddx agent workers stop`.\n\n5. **Start worker.** A \"Start worker\" button in the Workers page header opens a form (harness/profile, effort, optional label filter). Submitting fires a `startWorker` mutation. On success the new worker appears in the list within 2s. Form validation prevents empty submissions. The code path reuses `ddx work` / execute-loop internals — not a separate implementation.\n\n6. **Audit trail.** Every lifecycle action (start, stop, cancel) is recorded as a timestamped event visible on the worker detail page, including the actor identifier the server has for the requester.\n\n7. **No regressions.** Existing live log streaming, recent events, and phase overrides continue to work. Playwright smoke of the worker detail page passes.\n\n8. **Playwright e2e.**\n   - Navigates to Workers page, asserts header description.\n   - Clicks \"Start worker\", fills form, submits, asserts new row appears.\n   - Clicks Stop on that row, confirms, asserts row state transitions to `stopped`.\n   - Clicks into the stopped worker, asserts the Sessions section renders (or the empty state).\n   - Asserts the \"Sessions\" link in the worker page header navigates to the Sessions page.\n\n9. **Design review sign-off.** Before implementation begins, a short design note (one page or less) in `docs/helix/02-design/` captures the Workers-vs-Sessions mental model, the chosen controls, and any CLI primitives that need to exist. Implementation PR links to that note.","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:18:04.067488813Z","description":"## Observed\n\n`/nodes/.../projects/.../workers` renders a table of workers but has no buttons, no controls, and no explanation of how it differs from `/sessions`. The layout (`cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte`) shows ID / Kind / State / Current Bead / Attempts — read-only. Clicking a row drills into live-streaming logs and recent events, but offers no actions there either.\n\nThis raised a fair question: **what is this screen for, and how is it different from Sessions?** The answer isn't in the UI.\n\n## Ground truth (from the CLI)\n\n- **Worker** = a persistent execute-loop process draining the bead queue (`ddx work` / `ddx agent execute-loop`). Has a lifecycle: running / idle / stopped / error. Has a *current bead* and cumulative attempts/successes/failures. Can be stopped via `ddx agent workers stop`. Long-lived.\n- **Session** = a historical record of one agent invocation (harness, model, bead, tokens, cost, outcome, start/end). Immutable after completion. One worker produces many sessions over its lifetime.\n\nSo: Workers are *processes you operate*. Sessions are *events you review*. The current UI exposes both as flat tables and lets the reader guess which is which.\n\n## Gaps\n\n1. **No disambiguation.** No page-level copy, no link between the two, no worker→sessions navigation (\"show sessions produced by this worker\"), no session→worker backlink.\n2. **No lifecycle controls on Workers.** The CLI has `ddx agent workers stop` and `ddx work` (start). The UI has neither button. No stop, start, restart, or \"cancel current bead\" affordance. The State column is a spectator sport.\n3. **No worker start UX.** There is no way to start a queue drain from the UI — users must drop to a terminal.\n4. **Unclear empty state.** \"No workers found.\" does not say whether that is normal (nothing running) or broken (server can't see workers). No hint about how to start one.\n5. **No worker details beyond logs.** Worker detail page shows live phase + log tail + tool calls but not: worker config (project path, profile/harness, started-at, last-heartbeat), resource usage, or the list of sessions this worker has produced.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nSplit into two deliverables — do them together if we're touching this screen anyway, but they are independently landable:\n\n### A. Information architecture (copy + navigation)\n\n- Add a short header description to each page: \"Workers drain the bead queue. Sessions are the history of what they ran.\"\n- Mutual links: Workers page links to \"Recent sessions →\", Sessions page links to \"Workers →\".\n- On worker detail: a \"Sessions\" tab or section listing sessions where `session.workerId == this.id` (requires session records to carry workerID; check whether they do).\n- On session detail (expandable row already exists): if the session has a workerID, surface it as a link back to the worker.\n\n### B. Lifecycle controls\n\n- **Stop** button per running worker row → calls a GraphQL mutation that invokes the equivalent of `ddx agent workers stop \u003cid\u003e`. Confirms before acting.\n- **Start worker** button in the page header → opens a small form (project, profile/harness, effort, optional bead filter) and calls a `startWorker` mutation that shells out to `ddx work` in the background. The form should default to sane values and be submittable in one click for the common case.\n- **Cancel current bead** action on the worker detail page — signals the worker to abandon the in-flight bead without killing the loop. (Only if CLI primitive exists; otherwise descope and note in the bead.)\n- All mutations must be idempotent and log an audit record viewable in the UI (who did it, when). Reuse existing session-event plumbing if possible.\n\n### Out of scope\n\n- Rewriting execute-loop semantics.\n- Cross-project worker orchestration.\n- Auth / permissions model for who can stop workers (assume single-operator for now; file a follow-up if multi-operator becomes real).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:00:24.797884238Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. .ddx/beads.jsonl:241:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing what my agents have done, I open the Executions view, filter to a specific bead or verdict or harness, click into one, and see the prompt it received, the verdict it produced, the session it corresponds to (with cost + tokens), and every tool call it made — all without leaving the browser.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Sidebar entry.** \"Executions\" added to the project sidebar between \"Sessions\" and \"Personas\" (or wherever fits the existing visual order — design call; captured in the PR description). Active-highlight works.\n\n2. **List route.** `/executions` renders a paginated, filterable table with columns: timestamp, bead (id + title as link), harness/model, verdict (color-coded pass/block/error/other), duration, cost. Default sort: newest first. Filters: verdict, harness, bead, since/until date, free-text search over prompt first ~200 chars.\n\n3. **Detail route.** `/executions/\u003cid\u003e` shows five tabs as described (Manifest, Prompt, Result, Session, Tool calls). Tab switching doesn't re-fetch already-loaded data.\n\n4. **Tool calls pane.** Virtualized list. First 50 calls load with the page; subsequent pages load on scroll. Each entry shows tool name + collapsed input/output, expandable on click. Links to the raw agent-log file for operators who want to diff.\n\n5. **GraphQL completeness.** `executions`, `execution`, `executionToolCalls` resolvers exist and pass feat-008-style integration tests. Schema documented in `schema.graphql`.\n\n6. **Cross-links present.**\n   - Bead detail renders an \"Executions\" section listing linked executions with verdict + timestamp, each clicking into the detail route.\n   - Session detail shows \"Execution\" link when the session has one.\n   - Commits page shows execution link when commit sha == execution.result_rev.\n\n7. **Fixture + perf.**\n   - Seeded fixture: ≥1,000 executions, ≥100 with seeded tool-call streams (≥50 calls each).\n   - List view on this fixture: p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n   - Detail view (no tool calls): p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP.\n   - Tool-call page 1 (first 50 entries): p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.\n   - If any target cannot be met in-place, raise DB-substrate decision with user before switching.\n\n8. **Playwright e2e.**\n   - Seeds a project with 3 executions across 2 beads with mixed verdicts.\n   - Navigates to `/executions`, asserts the table renders 3 rows and filter-by-verdict narrows correctly.\n   - Clicks into one execution, asserts all 5 tabs exist and the Prompt tab shows the seeded prompt.\n   - Opens the Tool calls tab, expands an entry, asserts input/output rendering.\n   - Clicks the linked bead from the detail, asserts navigation to bead detail and that the Executions section on that bead lists this execution.\n\n9. **Depends on.**\n   - Soft dependency on `ddx-2ceb02fa` for the Session tab's cost/token numbers. If not yet landed, render \"not available\" for those fields and wire them in after.\n   - Adjacent: `ddx-6904a90b` (billing mode) — Session tab's cost shows the correct bucket once that lands.","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:55:34.427486388Z","description":"## Observed / Motivation\n\n`.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/{manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json}` bundles are already being produced per `ddx agent execute-bead` attempt — 469 of them on the current repo. They contain the canonical record of what an agent was asked to do and what verdict the attempt produced. **Nothing in the web UI surfaces them today.** A user who wants to ask \"why did this bead end up in this state?\" has to drop to the filesystem.\n\nSessions (`sessions.jsonl` / sharded index per `ddx-2ceb02fa`) carry the harness/cost/token telemetry but not the verdict and not the bead linkage. Agent log streams (`agent-*.jsonl`) carry the tool-call trace but are scattered across hundreds of files. **Executions are the natural join** — they connect a bead, a session, a prompt, a verdict, and a tool-call stream.\n\n## Ground truth\n\nPer-execution on disk:\n\n```\n.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/\n  manifest.json      { harness, model, base_rev, result_rev, verdict, bead_id, execution_dir }\n  prompt.md          the prompt that was sent\n  result.json        { verdict, rationale, ... }\n```\n\nRelations:\n- `manifest.bead_id` → bead\n- a session id is recorded in bead events (`kind:cost`, `kind:routing`) and in the agent-log stream; both can map back to an execution via correlation metadata\n- tool calls live in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` (and `agent-s-*.jsonl`), keyed by session id\n\n## Scope\n\n### Part 1 — GraphQL surface\n\n- `executions(projectId, first, after, beadId?, since?, until?, verdict?, harness?)` — paginated list of execution bundles.\n- `execution(id)` — one bundle, fully materialized: manifest fields, prompt markdown, result JSON, linked bead (id + title), linked session (id + harness + model + cost + token totals), tool-call stream pointer.\n- `executionToolCalls(id, first, after)` — paginated tool-call stream for the execution, read lazily from the agent-log file. Matches the event shape used by the workers detail page.\n\nExecutions source of truth: directory scan of `.ddx/executions/` filtered/paginated at the resolver. Shard discipline: follow the sessions-index monthly shard pattern (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) if listing all executions grows beyond tens of thousands — per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`, raise the storage decision with the user before switching substrate.\n\n### Part 2 — Sidebar + routes\n\n- Add \"Executions\" to the project sidebar (`NavShell.svelte`).\n- Routes:\n  - `/nodes/\u003cn\u003e/projects/\u003cp\u003e/executions` — list view with filters (verdict, harness, bead, date range, free-text over prompt). Table columns: timestamp, bead, harness/model, verdict (pass/block/error), duration, cost.\n  - `/nodes/\u003cn\u003e/projects/\u003cp\u003e/executions/\u003cid\u003e` — detail view with five tabs: **Manifest**, **Prompt** (rendered markdown), **Result** (rationale + raw JSON toggle), **Session** (harness, model, cost, token totals, link to `/sessions/\u003cid\u003e`), **Tool calls** (virtualized lazy-scroll list of tool_use + tool_result pairs with expandable payloads).\n\n### Part 3 — Cross-links (make the graph real)\n\n- **Bead detail:** new \"Executions\" section listing all executions for this bead with verdict + timestamp + link. Already have data: manifest.bead_id.\n- **Session detail:** new \"Execution\" link pointing to the execution this session ran for, when the session has a linked execution.\n- **Commits page:** already shows commits; if a commit was produced by an execution (result_rev == commit sha), surface the execution link.\n- **Graph/Docs pages:** out of scope for this bead.\n\n### Part 4 — Perf\n\n- Fixture: ≥1,000 executions, per standing directive `ddx-9ce6842a`. Tool-call streams seeded for 10% of them.\n- Targets: list view p95 ≤ 200ms HTTP, detail view without tool calls p95 ≤ 150ms HTTP, tool-call page-1 (first 50) p95 ≤ 300ms HTTP.\n- If these can't be met with filesystem scan + in-memory filter, raise DB-substrate decision with user per standing directive.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Writing new execution bundles (that's already happening).\n- Executions from other projects/nodes (keep per-project for v1).\n- Tool-call payload redaction / privacy. File a follow-up if relevant.\n- `.claude/worktrees/` worktree lifecycle tracking — covered by companion bead per user's split request.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:01:23.105870644Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. .ddx/beads.jsonl:263:{"acceptance":"Web UI route /nodes/:nodeId/agents (or /workers) lists all workers from /api/agent/workers with auto-refresh every ~2s. A worker row links to /nodes/:nodeId/agents/:workerId with three panels: prompt, log stream (structured events rendered with FormatSessionLogLines symbols), utilization (tokens/cost/duration/attempts). Both pages work against the alpha14 server without any additional backend work beyond optionally exposing the prompt content. Playwright test covers: list-shows-running-worker, list-refreshes-on-state-change, detail-renders-prompt-log-and-utilization.","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T08:13:37Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"c4c3fdf0cd73849b5432b36f3a99b835a4320021","created_at":"2026-04-14T03:45:23.096752985Z","description":"The server knows everything about running workers (state, harness, model, bead, attempts, prompt file, structured event log, token/cost/duration) via /api/agent/workers endpoints but the web UI has no live workers view. When an operator selects the eitri node they should land on (or reach in one click) an Agents page that lists every running worker from /api/agent/workers with bead-id, harness, model, state, elapsed, and latest status line. Clicking a row should open a worker detail page showing: (1) the rendered bead prompt from .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/prompt.md, (2) the structured session log stream from .ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-\u003csession\u003e.jsonl rendered via FormatSessionLogLines (so tool calls, turn counts, and timings are readable), (3) a session utilization panel with input/output/total tokens, cost-if-available, duration, attempts and successes/failures from the worker record, and (4) the base-rev and result-rev link once the worker has made progress. Polling at ~2s is fine for v1; no WebSocket required.\\n\\nBacking endpoints that already exist:\\n- GET /api/agent/workers -\u003e list\\n- GET /api/agent/workers/:id -\u003e detail\\n- GET /api/agent/workers/:id/log -\u003e stdout+stderr streams\\n\\nMissing backing endpoints (if not already there): GET /api/agent/workers/:id/prompt to serve prompt.md content without requiring the UI to know the execution bundle path; optionally /api/agent/workers/:id/events to serve the structured JSONL stream if /log doesn't already cover it.","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"success\nresult_rev=c4c3fdf0cd73849b5432b36f3a99b835a4320021\nbase_rev=946a094ca1988bea2586f7c45d9ed0e8b6a4d565","created_at":"2026-04-14T08:21:48.619128544Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"success"}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T08:21:37.455975215Z","id":"ddx-7be82986","issue_type":"task","owner":"ddx","parent":"ddx-2e8ad12b","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-c4c3fdf","status":"closed","title":"Dashboard UI: live workers page with per-worker prompt, log, and utilization detail","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:40.492802488Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:275:{"acceptance":"- [ ] New function gitPushLanded(projectRoot, defaultBranch) called after successful CloseWithEvidence in execute_bead_loop.go\n- [ ] Push uses --ff-only; never --force or --force-with-lease\n- [ ] Push rejection triggers land_conflict + unclaim + refs/ddx/iterations/ preservation + stop-the-loop\n- [ ] Network failure triggers 'push_pending' attempt marker but does NOT fail the bead; subsequent workers on the same branch see the pending state and halt\n- [ ] Unit tests: successful push, rejected push, network failure\n- [ ] Integration test: two workers racing, one wins the push, the other is rejected and unclaims its bead\n- [ ] Integration test: repeated loops push each successful bead immediately (origin advances after every success)\n- [ ] Docs updated: success path includes push; land_conflict path is the canonical 'two machines raced' outcome","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:19:32.504244855Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-83ec1430","depends_on_id":"ddx-bd42f640","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"}],"description":"Second child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. When a worker successfully lands a bead (status = success), the new commit sits on the local branch forever. There is no git push. The source in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_commit.go has ZERO references to push/fetch/origin (grep verified). Result: successful work on one machine is invisible to every other machine, and the local branch grows indefinitely with unsynced commits.\n\n## Evidence\n\ncli/internal/agent/execute_bead_commit.go defines the commit step but never calls git push. The only mention of 'git' in that file is a comment about trailers.\n\ncli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go at the success branch (around line 343):\n\n    if report.Status == ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess {\n        if err := w.Store.CloseWithEvidence(candidate.ID, report.SessionID, report.ResultRev); err != nil {\n            return result, err\n        }\n        result.Successes++\n        result.LastSuccessAt = now().UTC()\n    }\n\nThe tracker is updated (CloseWithEvidence writes to beads.jsonl) but the working tree / commits are never pushed to origin.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nAfter a successful landing (merge outcome = 'merged' AND bead status = success), the worker must:\n\n1. Run `git push --ff-only origin \u003cdefault-branch\u003e`\n2. On rejection:\n   - Classify as ExecuteBeadStatusLandConflict (this status already exists in execute_bead_status.go)\n   - Preserve the commits under refs/ddx/iterations/ (the infrastructure already exists for the preserved-result case)\n   - Unclaim the bead so a later attempt can pick it up again AFTER divergence is resolved\n   - Emit a 'push_rejected' event with the local HEAD and the remote tip\n   - Stop the worker loop (divergence requires human attention)\n3. On network failure: similar to fetch — log a warning, mark the attempt with a 'push_pending' flag, continue\n4. Pair this push with the fetch-first bead (sibling ddx-bd42f640) so the loop is: fetch → verify ancestry → run bead → push — a closed consistency loop\n\n## Why --ff-only\n\nForce-with-lease is tempting but wrong here — if push is rejected, it means another machine pushed in the meantime, and we want to detect that and reconcile, not overwrite. --ff-only ensures we only ever advance origin, never rewrite it. A rejection is a signal, not a problem to work around.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Fresh clone, worker lands a bead, push succeeds, origin advances to new HEAD\n- Another machine pushed between fetch and commit (race): second worker lands, push is rejected, bead is unclaimed and marked land_conflict, loop stops\n- Network unreachable: push is deferred, attempt records 'push_pending', worker continues but subsequent workers halt on the same branch\n- Default branch is configurable","id":"ddx-83ec1430","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","git","orchestration"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: push to origin on successful bead landing","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.60994229Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:285:{"acceptance":"- [ ] observability_workdir.go does not build a Runner internally\n- [ ] Runner.ProbeHarnessState, Runner.LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, Runner.RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, Runner.RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux methods deleted\n- [ ] Runner struct fields (Registry, Discovery, Catalog, AgentProvider, etc.) — only those needed by REMAINING test scaffolding kept; rest deleted\n- [ ] Build clean; existing tests pass\n- [ ] After this lands, the 5 file-delete beads execute cleanly","closing_commit_sha":"f1510701e2cea4e56a09ba987598a91ec4bd7dee","created_at":"2026-04-19T01:50:31.887099122Z","description":"Surfaced by ddx-bc1e99ec (commit 280f58e0). The observability_workdir.go wrappers currently build a minimal Runner via NewRunner(cfg) and call its observability methods (ProbeHarnessState, LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux). This keeps Runner alive as an internal implementation detail.\n\nGoal: lift the file-reading logic OUT of Runner methods into free functions (or just inline into observability_workdir.go). Then Runner can finally die, and the 5 file-delete beads cascade.\n\nKey insight: the observability data DDx surfaces lives in DDx-managed paths (session JSONLs in cfg.SessionLogDir, quota caches in agent module - need to call into agent for quota refresh). These are file-read operations, not Runner-state operations.\n\nFiles involved:\n- cli/internal/agent/observability_workdir.go — currently uses Runner-build pattern; rewrite to direct file reads\n- cli/internal/agent/runner.go — extract file-reading logic from methods then delete those methods (Runner.ProbeHarnessState, Runner.LoadRoutingSignalSnapshot, Runner.RefreshClaudeQuotaViaTmux, Runner.RefreshCodexQuotaViaTmux). The methods themselves are likely just thin wrappers around Runner.Config + free helpers — extract the helpers, drop the methods.\n- For tmux quota refresh: call agent module helpers directly (internal/harnesses/{claude,codex}/quota_tmux.go via the existing tmux probe ENTRY POINTS; the agent has these functions exported, e.g. ReadClaudeQuotaViaTmux from internal/harnesses/claude — check)\n\nAfter this lands:\n- Runner public methods all gone\n- Runner struct can delete (Phase 5g — separate bead OR fold here)\n- The 5 file-delete beads (ddx-f0cab7b3 state+registry, ddx-ea7f041c routing.go, ddx-70c48576 routing observability, ddx-1bf6c0df tier_escalation, ddx-2912e7cd discovery+deadline+probe) cascade cleanly","id":"ddx-88bbd24d","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:build","area:agent","kind:cleanup"],"parent":"ddx-ac5c7fdb","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"Phase 5f: rewrite observability_workdir.go to read files directly; eliminate internal Runner usage","updated_at":"2026-04-19T02:13:13.387126743Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:287:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator using a browser — on a machine without a terminal, or a phone, or a laptop away from my dev box — I can open my project's home page, type a prompt into a chat pane, and see the DDx agent work on it in real time. Tool calls stream as they happen, costs accrue visibly, and the conversation persists so I can come back to it.\n\n**Acceptance criteria (written for Option A; rewrite if design note chooses B/C/D):**\n\n1. **Design note landed.** One-pager in `docs/helix/02-design/` records the chosen option, the answers to the six subsidiary questions, and the message/thread schema. Implementation PR links to it.\n\n2. **Thread model.** Chat threads persist under `.ddx/chat/\u003cthread-id\u003e/{manifest.json,messages.jsonl}`. One thread per project per operator by default; operators can create named threads. Messages.jsonl is append-only, one JSON object per message (user or assistant), with `sessionId` linking each assistant turn back to an entry in the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`).\n\n3. **GraphQL surface.** New resolvers:\n   - Query: `chatThreads(projectId)`, `chatThread(id)`, `chatMessages(threadId, since?)`.\n   - Mutation: `chatThreadCreate(projectId, title?)`, `chatSend(threadId, message, harness?, profile?)` → kicks off a session via `service.Execute`.\n   - Subscription: `chatStream(threadId)` → emits assistant message chunks + tool call events (same event vocabulary as `workers.recentEvents`).\n\n4. **Home page UI.** Project home page gains a chat pane below the actions section. Pane shows thread list, active thread messages, input box, harness/profile picker. Tool calls render inline within assistant messages. Streaming updates as chunks arrive.\n\n5. **Session linkage.** Each assistant turn creates one session in the sessions index. Clicking an assistant message shows the session id and a link to the session detail (`/sessions/\u003cid\u003e`). Chat thread header shows cumulative session count + total cost for the thread.\n\n6. **Concurrency.** Two browsers open on the same thread see each other's messages within 2s (subscription-driven). Two operators on different threads in the same project do not interfere.\n\n7. **Interrupt.** The in-flight assistant turn can be cancelled via a Stop button; server sends the harness the equivalent of `ddx agent workers stop \u003cid\u003e` for the in-flight session. The thread records a cancelled turn, does not try to auto-resume.\n\n8. **Cost guardrail (soft).** If the cumulative thread cost passes a configurable ceiling (`.ddx/config.yaml: chat.cost_ceiling_usd`, default unset), the UI warns but does not block. Hard block is a follow-up.\n\n9. **Auth posture.**\n   - Per design note. If the chosen posture is \"single-operator localhost / tsnet only for v1,\" the chat routes are served unauthenticated behind tsnet and the design note records the gap to close before broader deployment.\n   - A small middleware gate is in place even for v1 so adding auth later is schema-local, not route-local.\n\n10. **Playwright e2e.**\n    - Seeds a project with zero chat threads.\n    - Navigates to project home, asserts the chat pane renders with \"Start a new thread\" affordance.\n    - Starts a thread, sends a short prompt, simulates a streamed session response with one tool call, asserts chunks appear in order and the session id link is present.\n    - Opens a second browser context on the same thread, asserts both see new messages within 2s.\n    - Cancels an in-flight turn, asserts the cancelled state is persisted.\n\n11. **Depends on.**\n    - `ddx-2513f08c` (home page reachable from sidebar) — hard dependency.\n    - `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index) — hard dependency for the session linkage + cost rollup.\n\n12. **Explicitly deferred.** PTY integration (Option B / pop-out terminal from Option C) is its own bead, filed only if the Option A rollout surfaces real interactive-steering needs.","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:51:38.374211227Z","description":"## Observed / Motivation\n\nUser wants to \"lift work out of the TUI entirely and allow non-tmux users to work remotely.\" Today the only way to prompt the DDx agent stack interactively is a terminal session. The web UI is read-mostly: dispatch three actions (drain/align/checks) and review results. There is no surface that lets an operator say \"go do X\" against the project in natural language from a browser.\n\nRelated UX: the project home page already exists (`/nodes/.../projects/\u003cid\u003e/`), renders queue summary + actions, and is the natural host for a chat affordance. First the home page needs to be reachable from the sidebar (`ddx-2513f08c`) — this bead depends on that work.\n\n## The tension\n\nThe DDx harness abstraction (`cli/internal/agent/service_run.go`, `service.Execute`) is designed for **non-interactive** use: prompt in, structured result out, optional streaming. This is the right shape for queue-draining, quorum, evaluation, and automation. It is **not** the shape of a TUI conversation with tab completion, mid-run steering, and back-channel signals.\n\nAn operator-facing chat can land on either side of that line. The bead records all four options so the design phase can pick one deliberately.\n\n## Options (design phase decides, recommended path noted)\n\n### Option A — Chat over the harness abstraction (RECOMMENDED starting point)\n\n- Chat UI posts a prompt → server dispatches a session via `service.Execute` (or a wrapper) → streams tool calls + response chunks back over a GraphQL subscription.\n- Message history is persisted as a thread; each user turn = one new session. Responses reuse the existing `recentEvents` stream shape already used on the workers detail page.\n- Works with every harness today because the harness abstraction is what it is. claude, codex, agent (embedded) all participate.\n- **Trade-off:** no mid-run steering, no tab completion, no live back-and-forth inside one agent invocation.\n- Matches the user's stated target audience (non-TUI users who want remote browser access). Those users aren't trying to steer a TUI — they're trying to leave it behind.\n\n### Option B — PTY gateway per harness\n\n- Server owns a PTY pool. Each chat thread attaches to one harness process (claude/codex/etc.) over a WebSocket; browser renders xterm.js.\n- Full interactivity, including TUI steering.\n- **Trade-off:** PTY lifecycle, auth, resource limits, tab-close-loses-session semantics, multi-user access control — all real. The harness abstraction is bypassed for this mode, which creates a second execution path to maintain.\n\n### Option C — Hybrid (chat-first + pop-out terminal)\n\n- Option A is the primary surface. When a chat reaches a point that needs interactive steering, a \"Open terminal\" escape hatch attaches a PTY to the current harness session.\n- **Trade-off:** more surface area, but honest about both use cases. Most sessions stay on the cheap path.\n\n### Option D — Web-tmux / ttyd wrapper\n\n- Embed the existing terminal tooling directly: ttyd or xterm.js over a tmux socket owned by the server.\n- **Trade-off:** fastest to ship, zero harness work, but the UX is a terminal in a browser — not \"chat.\" Requires tmux. Doesn't serve the stated \"non-tmux users\" audience.\n\n### Recommendation\n\nStart with **A**. If the design note documents real interactive-steering needs we can't paper over, the right follow-up is **C** (A + PTY escape hatch), not B (PTY-only).\n\n## Scope of this bead\n\n- Design note in `docs/helix/02-design/` locking the option, the message schema, persistence model, and auth posture. Implementation PR links to it.\n- First implementation pass: Option A.\n- Home page gains a chat pane as its primary interaction surface, alongside the existing queue summary + actions (which stay — they're quick-dispatch affordances, not replaced by chat).\n\n## Subsidiary questions the design note must answer\n\n1. **Message persistence.** Are chat threads per-project or per-user? Stored where (`.ddx/chat/\u003cthread-id\u003e/` or elsewhere)? Is history shared across operators on the same project, or isolated per operator identity?\n2. **Harness selection.** Does the chat pick a harness per-thread (picker in the UI) or per-message (re-route each turn)? Default to profile-based selection?\n3. **Cost accountability.** Every chat message spawns a session. Sessions are already tracked in the sessions index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). The chat thread should surface cumulative cost to the operator so they don't blow budget unknowingly.\n4. **Tool execution scope.** Chat prompts will trigger agent tool calls that read/write the project tree. Is the chat session bounded to the same permissions as `ddx agent run`? Does it run in the project worktree, a worktree per thread, a scratch dir? Default safe answer: same worktree as `ddx agent run` (user trusts ddx running anyway), but design note records it.\n5. **Concurrency.** What happens if two operators have the chat open on the same project and both send messages? Default: independent threads, each with its own session — no shared state.\n6. **Auth.** Today the server assumes single-operator localhost. Remote use implies some identity concept. Design note picks a posture: delegate to reverse proxy, ship a simple token, or punt and require tsnet + single-operator for v1.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Full PTY implementation (Option B). Follow-up bead if A+hybrid doesn't suffice.\n- Cross-project chat.\n- Voice input.","feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-88e91fdb","issue_type":"epic","labels":["feat-008","feat-006","chat","remote-ops","design"],"priority":2,"status":"open","title":"Add chat interface to project home page: prompt ddx remotely without the TUI","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:51:38.374211227Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:299:{"acceptance":"ddx agent workers prints a table with STATE, BEAD ID, TITLE, HARNESS, MODEL, ELAPSED, ATTEMPTS columns. Server-submitted workers show state from WorkerManager. Local --local workers are detected via manifest scan of .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories. --json flag emits raw JSON. --watch re-renders every 2s. A worker executing bead axon-abc123 shows that bead ID and its title (truncated). An idle system (no active workers) prints 'no active workers' rather than an empty table. No dependency on worktrunk or any external binary beyond git.","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T07:31:03Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"3f16c49fc95957923d3f45fc2a93ec647ba859b4","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:34:44.901809653Z","description":"There is no CLI command to see which workers are running and which bead each is executing. The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Harness, WorkerRecord.Model, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n  --json         emit raw JSON array\n  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, harness, model, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n  - Read execute-loop-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | HARNESS | MODEL | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"success\nresult_rev=3f16c49fc95957923d3f45fc2a93ec647ba859b4\nbase_rev=93d4b5651cc11827025d29f1c6c099b63cb163c4","created_at":"2026-04-14T07:45:16.912491783Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"success"}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T07:45:03.73510309Z","id":"ddx-9060c8b2","issue_type":"task","labels":["ux","execute-loop","observability","cli"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-3f16c49","status":"closed","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:47.499704451Z"}

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n\\n#[test]\\nfn jwt_roundtrip_succeeds() {\\n    let issuer = JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER_ID.to_string());\\n    let claims = fn error_not_bearer_scheme() {\\n    let state = make_state();\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .uri(TEST_PATH)\\n        .header(\\\"Authorization\\\", assert_eq!(error_code(\u0026json), \\\"credential_malformed\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/// Row 2b: JWT structurally invalid → 401 credential_malformed\\n#[tokio::test]\\nasync fn error_jwt_malformed() {\\n    let \\\"orders\\\";\\nconst USER_ID: \u0026str = \\\"u-integration-01\\\";\\nconst SECRET: \u0026[u8] = b\\\"integration-test-secret\\\";\\nconst PROTECTED_PATH: \u0026str = \\\"/tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping\\\";\\n\\nfn now_secs() -\u003e u64 {\\n    SystemTime::now()\\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\\n        .unwrap_or_default()\\n        .as_secs()\\n}\\n\\nfn make_state() -\u003e Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e {\\n    let mut storage = MemoryStorageAdapter::default();\\n    storage.insert_user(User {\\n        id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        display_name: \\\"Integration User\\\".to_string(),\\n        email: None,\\n        created_at_ms: 0,\\n        suspended_at_ms: None,\\n    });\\n    storage.insert_tenant_member(TenantMember {\\n        tenant_id: TenantId::new(TENANT),\\n        user_id: UserId::new(USER_ID),\\n        role: TenantRole::Write,\\n    });\\n    Arc::new(AuthPipelineState {\\n        issuer: Arc::new(JwtIssuer::new(SECRET.to_vec(), ISSUER.to_string())),\\n        revocation_cache: Arc::new(InMemoryRevocationCache::new()),\\n        storage: Arc::new(Mutex::new(\\n            Box::new(storage) as Box\u003cdyn axon_storage::StorageAdapter + Send + Sync\u003e\\n        )),\\n    })\\n}\\n\\nfn make_app(state: Arc\u003cAuthPipelineState\u003e) -\u003e Router {\\n    Router::new()\\n        .route(PROTECTED_PATH, get(|| async { StatusCode::OK }))\\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\\n            state,\\n            jwt_verify_layer,\\n        ))\\n}\\n\\nasync fn send(app: Router, req: Request\u003cBody\u003e) -\u003e (StatusCode, Value) {\\n    let resp = app.oneshot(req).await.expect(\\\"service should not fail\\\");\\n    let status = resp.status();\\n    let bytes = axum::body::to_bytes(resp.into_body(), 64 * 1024)\\n        .await\\n        .unwrap_or_default();\\n    let json: Value = serde_json::from_slice(\u0026bytes).unwrap_or(Value::Null);\\n    (status, json)\\n}\\n\\nfn bearer(token: \u0026str) -\u003e String {\\n    format!(\\\"Bearer {token}\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn valid_token(issuer: \u0026JwtIssuer, grants: Grants) -\u003e String {\\n    let now = now_secs();\\n    let claims = JwtClaims {\\n        iss: ISSUER.to_string(),\\n        sub: USER_ID.to_string(),\\n        aud: TENANT.to_string(),\\n        jti: Uuid::now_v7().to_string(),\\n        iat: now,\\n        nbf: now,\\n        exp: now + 3600,\\n        grants,\\n    };\\n    issuer.issue(\u0026claims).expect(\\\"issue should succeed\\\")\\n}\\n\\nfn orders_read_grants() -\u003e Grants {\\n    Grants {\\n        databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {\\n            name: DATABASE.to_string(),\\n            ops: vec![Op::Read],\\n        }],\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test 1: No auth header → 401 unauthenticated Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n        .body(Body::empty())\\n        .unwrap();\\n    let (status, json) = send(app, req).await;\\n    assert_eq!(status, StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED);\\n    assert_eq!(\\n        json[\\\"error\\\"][\\\"code\\\"].as_str().unwrap_or(\\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"unauthenticated\\\"\\n    );\\n}\\n\\n// ── Test token = valid_token(\u0026state.issuer, orders_read_grants());\\n    let app = make_app(state);\\n    let req = Request::builder()\\n        .method(\\\"GET\\\")\\n        .uri(PROTECTED_PATH)\\n directory for this skill: /home/erik/.claude/skills/helix-commit\\n\\n# Commit: HELIX-Compliant Git Commit\\n\\nCreate a well-formatted commit with issue traceability, run the project's\\nbuild gate, push, and optionally update the tracker.\\n\\n## When to Use\\n\\n- After implementing a tracked issue\\n- When you need a properly formatted commit message\\n- When codex or another agent needs commit support\\n\\n## What It Does\\n\\n1. **Inspect** staged changes (`git diff --cached --stat`)\\n2. **Format** commit message:\\n   - First line: `\u003cissue-id\u003e \u003cconcise summary\u003e` (under 72 chars)\\n   - Body: what changed and why, governing artifact refs\\n   - Trailer: verification commands run\\n3. **Pre-push gate**: run the project's build check before pushing\\n   - `lefthook run pre-commit` if available\\n   - `cargo check --workspace` for Rust projects\\n   - `npm test` for Node.js projects\\n4. **Commit and push**: `git commit`, `git pull --rebase`, `git push`\\n5. **Tracker update** (if issue-id provided): close the issue\\n\\n## Commit Message Format\\n\\n```\\nhx-abc123 Short summary of the change\\n\\nLonger description of what changed and why. Reference governing\\nartifacts when relevant (SD-001, TP-002, etc.).\\n\\nVerification: cargo test -p changed-crate, cargo clippy\\n```\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\n```bash\\nhelix commit hx-abc123      # commit, gate, push, close issue\\nhelix commit                 # commit and gate without tracker update\\n```\\n\"}]},\"parent_tool_use_id\":null,\"session_id\":\"80f0eb9d-4064-4d06-8560-e247ce1170f2\",\"uuid\":\"389e7b5b-4f0d-4836-a072-b1bc977baa31\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-04-15T01:49:25.805Z\",\"isSynthetic\":true}\n{\"type\":\"assistant\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"id\":\"msg_01P2d4aV4hoZu9dRSEoGCXwm\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"thinking\",\"thinking\":\"Now I'll perform the helix commit following the skill instructions. .ddx/executions/20260418T061704-ff2adfb4/prompt.md:54:+    "description": "The embedded ddx-agent harness honors the per-run SessionLogDir override (agent_runner.go:136-138: `logDir := opts.SessionLogDir; if empty fall back to r.Config.SessionLogDir`) so that execute-bead runs drop their JSONL trace inside the bundle at .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/embedded/agent-*.jsonl.\n\nThe claude harness does NOT honor that override. claude_stream.go:327 reads only r.Config.SessionLogDir, so every claude execute-bead run writes its trace into the runner-wide default (`.ddx/agent-logs/`) and the bundle's embedded/ dir stays empty. execute_bead.go:446 already passes SessionLogDir: embeddedStateDir through RunOptions; it just isn't used by the claude path.\n\nEmpirical evidence from axon's 2026-04-15 ADR-018 session: 2 runs with `harness: agent` produced 34-51 MB traces in embedded/; 23 runs with `harness: claude` left embedded/ empty while `.ddx/agent-logs/` grew to 162 MB.\n\nFix: plumb opts.SessionLogDir into runClaudeStreaming (either accept it as a parameter or read it off resolvedOpts) and prefer it over r.Config.SessionLogDir when non-empty. Matches the embedded harness behavior exactly.\n\nParity matters: users want per-run forensic traces attached to the bundle regardless of which harness ran the bead.",
.ddx/executions/20260418T061704-ff2adfb4/prompt.md:55:+    "acceptance": "- [ ] claude_stream.go:runClaudeStreaming uses opts.SessionLogDir when non-empty, falling back to r.Config.SessionLogDir (matches agent_runner.go:136-138 pattern)\n- [ ] A claude-harness execute-bead run writes agent-\u003csessionID\u003e.jsonl into the bundle's embedded/ dir, not .ddx/agent-logs/\n- [ ] Unit test covers the override precedence for the claude path\n- [ ] Regression test: existing claude runs without execute-bead context still write to DefaultLogDir\n- [ ] Any other harnesses that emit JSONL traces (gemini, codex, etc.) audited and brought to the same override-respecting behavior",
.ddx/executions/20260422T205914-9451fa0a/manifest.json:13:    "title": "Sessions UI is blind to post-2026-04-19 activity: service.Execute path no longer writes sessions.jsonl",
.ddx/executions/20260422T205914-9451fa0a/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Observed\n\nThe Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.\n\n## Root cause\n\nThe Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.\n\nThe only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`\n- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`\n- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`\n\nProduction execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.\n\n## Secondary issue (same fix should address)\n\n`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nDo not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:\n\n1. **Decide the source of truth for \"a session\".** Current candidates on disk:\n   - `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.\n   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.\n   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).\n   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.\n\n2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.\n\n3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.\n\n4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.\n\n5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.\n- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.",
.ddx/executions/20260422T205914-9451fa0a/manifest.json:15:    "acceptance": "**User story:** As an operator, I expect the Sessions page to reflect reality — if I ran `ddx work` today, today's sessions appear within seconds. And I expect the feed not to silently break again the next time the agent execution path is refactored.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Session-index source of truth.** A single writer (invoked from `service.Execute` or a thin wrapper that every production agent-execution path calls) appends one line per completed agent invocation to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`. Fields are pointer-only: id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath (relative), nativeLogRef (relative). No prompt or response bodies embedded.\n\n2. **Single-call-site guarantee.** Unit test enumerates every production code path that executes an agent (execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, replay if still present) and asserts each appends exactly one index row. If a new path is added later without wiring this up, the test fails.\n\n3. **Visibility restored.** GraphQL `sessions` query reads `sessions.index.jsonl`. Running `ddx work` (or any agent-execution path) against a fixture project causes the new session to appear in a GraphQL query within 2 seconds of completion. E2E test demonstrates this.\n\n4. **Body lazy-load.** A new resolver `session(id: ID!) { prompt response stderr }` loads the heavy fields on demand from the execution bundle (`.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/`) or the agent-log path, without reading every session in the index.\n\n5. **Legacy migration.** A one-shot command (e.g. `ddx agent log reindex`) seeds the index from the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (397 MB corpus on the current repo), preserving historical sessions. Integration test uses a fixture `sessions.jsonl` and asserts the index has one row per legacy entry with all pointer fields populated.\n\n6. **Deprecated-file behavior.** After migration, the legacy `sessions.jsonl` is not read by the server. `ddx agent usage` and related CLI commands (`cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:197,246,348`, `cli/cmd/status.go:179`) are updated to read from the index too, verified by their existing tests.\n\n7. **Perf sanity.** The GraphQL `sessions` list resolves in ≤100ms p95 on a fixture with 10,000 index entries; current behavior times out or OOMs. Benchmark captured in bead notes as baseline + after-fix.\n\n8. **UI note.** If there is a gap in the index (e.g. the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap on the current repo, assuming backfill from executions/agent-logs is not feasible), the Sessions page surfaces a single-line banner \"No sessions recorded between X and Y\" so the gap is not silently invisible. If backfill is feasible, do it instead and skip the banner.\n\n9. **Playwright e2e.**\n   - Seeds a fixture project with `.ddx/executions/` bundles from both before and after a simulated gap.\n   - Runs the migration command.\n   - Opens `/sessions`, asserts the most recent session timestamp matches the latest bundle, asserts a clicked row lazy-loads prompt/response from the bundle rather than from an embedded payload.\n   - Asserts the feed updates live (within 2s) after a new simulated session is written.",

.ddx/executions/20260418T060325-e4e5ad24/prompt.md:5:The embedded ddx-agent harness honors the per-run SessionLogDir override (agent_runner.go:136-138: `logDir := opts.SessionLogDir; if empty fall back to r.Config.SessionLogDir`) so that execute-bead runs drop their JSONL trace inside the bundle at .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl.

The claude harness does NOT honor that override. claude_stream.go:327 reads only r.Config.SessionLogDir, so every claude execute-bead run writes its trace into the runner-wide default (`.ddx/agent-logs/`) and the bundle's embedded/ dir stays empty. execute_bead.go:446 already passes SessionLogDir: embeddedStateDir through RunOptions; it just isn't used by the claude path.

Empirical evidence from axon's 2026-04-15 ADR-018 session: 2 runs with `harness: agent` produced 34-51 MB traces in embedded/; 23 runs with `harness: claude` left embedded/ empty while `.ddx/agent-logs/` grew to 162 MB.

Fix: plumb opts.SessionLogDir into runClaudeStreaming (either accept it as a parameter or read it off resolvedOpts) and prefer it over r.Config.SessionLogDir when non-empty. Matches the embedded harness behavior exactly.

Parity matters: users want per-run forensic traces attached to the bundle regardless of which harness ran the bead.

Matches the embedded harness behavior exactly.\n\nParity matters: users want per-run forensic traces attached to the bundle regardless of which harness ran the bead.",
.ddx/executions/20260417T182339-81f075dd/manifest.json:15:    "acceptance": "- [ ] claude_stream.go:runClaudeStreaming uses opts.SessionLogDir when non-empty, falling back to r.Config.SessionLogDir (matches agent_runner.go:136-138 pattern)\n- [ ] A claude-harness execute-bead run writes agent-\u003csessionID\u003e.jsonl into the bundle's embedded/ dir, not .ddx/agent-logs/\n- [ ] Unit test covers the override precedence for the claude path\n- [ ] Regression test: existing claude runs without execute-bead context still write to DefaultLogDir\n- [ ] Any other harnesses that emit JSONL traces (gemini, codex, etc.) audited and brought to the same override-respecting behavior",
.ddx/executions/20260417T182339-81f075dd/prompt.md:5:The embedded ddx-agent harness honors the per-run SessionLogDir override (agent_runner.go:136-138: `logDir := opts.SessionLogDir; if empty fall back to r.Config.SessionLogDir`) so that execute-bead runs drop their JSONL trace inside the bundle at .ddx/executions/<attempt>/embedded/agent-*.jsonl.

The claude harness does NOT honor that override. claude_stream.go:327 reads only r.Config.SessionLogDir, so every claude execute-bead run writes its trace into the runner-wide default (`.ddx/agent-logs/`) and the bundle's embedded/ dir stays empty. execute_bead.go:446 already passes SessionLogDir: embeddedStateDir through RunOptions; it just isn't used by the claude path.

Empirical evidence from axon's 2026-04-15 ADR-018 session: 2 runs with `harness: agent` produced 34-51 MB traces in embedded/; 23 runs with `harness: claude` left embedded/ empty while `.ddx/agent-logs/` grew to 162 MB.

Fix: plumb opts.SessionLogDir into runClaudeStreaming (either accept it as a parameter or read it off resolvedOpts) and prefer it over r.Config.SessionLogDir when non-empty. Matches the embedded harness behavior exactly.

Parity matters: users want per-run forensic traces attached to the bundle regardless of which harness ran the bead.
