.ddx/executions/20260422T205937-3000fe01/prompt.md:5:## Observed

`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:

- **6,936 named "001"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.
- **344 named "002"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.
- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).
- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are "alive" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.
- State file size: 1.8 MB.

## Root cause

1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.
2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.
3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.

## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)

Both `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.

## Proposed direction

Three independently-landable fixes:

### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)

- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).
- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.
- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.

### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)

- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.
- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\w+\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.
- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.

### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI

- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-<timestamp>`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).
- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.
- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.

## Out of scope

- Moving state to SQLite.
- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)
- Renaming "001" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.
.ddx/executions/20260422T205917-a480e9d5/manifest.json:13:    "title": "Sessions UI is blind to post-2026-04-19 activity: service.Execute path no longer writes sessions.jsonl",
.ddx/executions/20260422T205917-a480e9d5/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Observed\n\nThe Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.\n\n## Root cause\n\nThe Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.\n\nThe only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`\n- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`\n- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`\n\nProduction execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.\n\n## Secondary issue (same fix should address)\n\n`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nDo not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:\n\n1. **Decide the source of truth for \"a session\".** Current candidates on disk:\n   - `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.\n   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.\n   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).\n   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.\n\n2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.\n\n3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.\n\n4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.\n\n5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.\n- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.",
.ddx/executions/20260422T205917-a480e9d5/manifest.json:15:    "acceptance": "**User story:** As an operator, I expect the Sessions page to reflect reality — if I ran `ddx work` today, today's sessions appear within seconds. And I expect the feed not to silently break again the next time the agent execution path is refactored.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Session-index source of truth.** A single writer (invoked from `service.Execute` or a thin wrapper that every production agent-execution path calls) appends one line per completed agent invocation to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`. Fields are pointer-only: id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath (relative), nativeLogRef (relative). No prompt or response bodies embedded.\n\n2. **Single-call-site guarantee.** Unit test enumerates every production code path that executes an agent (execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, replay if still present) and asserts each appends exactly one index row. If a new path is added later without wiring this up, the test fails.\n\n3. **Visibility restored.** GraphQL `sessions` query reads `sessions.index.jsonl`. Running `ddx work` (or any agent-execution path) against a fixture project causes the new session to appear in a GraphQL query within 2 seconds of completion. E2E test demonstrates this.\n\n4. **Body lazy-load.** A new resolver `session(id: ID!) { prompt response stderr }` loads the heavy fields on demand from the execution bundle (`.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/`) or the agent-log path, without reading every session in the index.\n\n5. **Legacy migration.** A one-shot command (e.g. `ddx agent log reindex`) seeds the index from the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (397 MB corpus on the current repo), preserving historical sessions. Integration test uses a fixture `sessions.jsonl` and asserts the index has one row per legacy entry with all pointer fields populated.\n\n6. **Deprecated-file behavior.** After migration, the legacy `sessions.jsonl` is not read by the server. `ddx agent usage` and related CLI commands (`cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:197,246,348`, `cli/cmd/status.go:179`) are updated to read from the index too, verified by their existing tests.\n\n7. **Perf sanity.** The GraphQL `sessions` list resolves in ≤100ms p95 on a fixture with 10,000 index entries; current behavior times out or OOMs. Benchmark captured in bead notes as baseline + after-fix.\n\n8. **UI note.** If there is a gap in the index (e.g. the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap on the current repo, assuming backfill from executions/agent-logs is not feasible), the Sessions page surfaces a single-line banner \"No sessions recorded between X and Y\" so the gap is not silently invisible. If backfill is feasible, do it instead and skip the banner.\n\n9. **Playwright e2e.**\n   - Seeds a fixture project with `.ddx/executions/` bundles from both before and after a simulated gap.\n   - Runs the migration command.\n   - Opens `/sessions`, asserts the most recent session timestamp matches the latest bundle, asserts a clicked row lazy-loads prompt/response from the bundle rather than from an embedded payload.\n   - Asserts the feed updates live (within 2s) after a new simulated session is written.",
.ddx/executions/20260422T205917-a480e9d5/prompt.md:3:    <title>Sessions UI is blind to post-2026-04-19 activity: service.Execute path no longer writes sessions.jsonl</title>
.ddx/executions/20260422T205917-a480e9d5/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.

## Root cause

The Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.

The only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:

- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)
- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`
- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`
- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`

Production execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.

## Secondary issue (same fix should address)

`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).

## Proposed direction

Do not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:

1. **Decide the source of truth for "a session".** Current candidates on disk:
   - `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.
   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.
   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).
   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.

2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.

3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.

4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.

5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).

## Out of scope

- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.
- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.
.ddx/executions/20260422T205917-a480e9d5/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As an operator, I expect the Sessions page to reflect reality — if I ran `ddx work` today, today's sessions appear within seconds. And I expect the feed not to silently break again the next time the agent execution path is refactored.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Session-index source of truth.** A single writer (invoked from `service.Execute` or a thin wrapper that every production agent-execution path calls) appends one line per completed agent invocation to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`. Fields are pointer-only: id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath (relative), nativeLogRef (relative). No prompt or response bodies embedded.

2. **Single-call-site guarantee.** Unit test enumerates every production code path that executes an agent (execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, replay if still present) and asserts each appends exactly one index row. If a new path is added later without wiring this up, the test fails.

3. **Visibility restored.** GraphQL `sessions` query reads `sessions.index.jsonl`. Running `ddx work` (or any agent-execution path) against a fixture project causes the new session to appear in a GraphQL query within 2 seconds of completion. E2E test demonstrates this.

4. **Body lazy-load.** A new resolver `session(id: ID!) { prompt response stderr }` loads the heavy fields on demand from the execution bundle (`.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/`) or the agent-log path, without reading every session in the index.

5. **Legacy migration.** A one-shot command (e.g. `ddx agent log reindex`) seeds the index from the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (397 MB corpus on the current repo), preserving historical sessions. Integration test uses a fixture `sessions.jsonl` and asserts the index has one row per legacy entry with all pointer fields populated.

6. **Deprecated-file behavior.** After migration, the legacy `sessions.jsonl` is not read by the server. `ddx agent usage` and related CLI commands (`cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:197,246,348`, `cli/cmd/status.go:179`) are updated to read from the index too, verified by their existing tests.

7. **Perf sanity.** The GraphQL `sessions` list resolves in ≤100ms p95 on a fixture with 10,000 index entries; current behavior times out or OOMs. Benchmark captured in bead notes as baseline + after-fix.

8. **UI note.** If there is a gap in the index (e.g. the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap on the current repo, assuming backfill from executions/agent-logs is not feasible), the Sessions page surfaces a single-line banner "No sessions recorded between X and Y" so the gap is not silently invisible. If backfill is feasible, do it instead and skip the banner.

9. **Playwright e2e.**
   - Seeds a fixture project with `.ddx/executions/` bundles from both before and after a simulated gap.
   - Runs the migration command.
   - Opens `/sessions`, asserts the most recent session timestamp matches the latest bundle, asserts a clicked row lazy-loads prompt/response from the bundle rather than from an embedded payload.
   - Asserts the feed updates live (within 2s) after a new simulated session is written.
.ddx/executions/20260422T205917-a480e9d5/prompt.md:11:## 2026-04-22 — User-directed amendment: shard the index

Per user feedback: a single `sessions.index.jsonl` just replays the same problem at smaller scale. The aggregate needs to be split.

### Revised storage shape

- Directory: `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/` (per-project, under each registered project's `.ddx`).
- Inside: **monthly shards** named `sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`.
- Each shard is pointer-only, same fields as the previously-proposed index (id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath, nativeLogRef).
- Writer resolves the target shard from `time.Now().UTC()` at append time and creates the shard file lazily on first write of the month.
- Heavy bodies (prompt, response, stderr) stay where they already live — `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` and agent-log streams — and are lazy-loaded by the `session(id:)` resolver.

### Why monthly, not daily / size-based / SQLite

- **Daily** — produces tens of thousands of files over the project lifetime; ReadDir becomes the cost.
- **Size-based** — requires coordinating rotation with the append; race-prone and hard to test.
- **SQLite** — overkill for first pass; revisit if a single monthly shard grows past tens of MB.
- **Monthly** — ≤12 shards/year, human-readable filenames, date-range queries map trivially to shard selection, bounded shard size in practice.

### Reader behavior

- Default Sessions query loads the **current + previous shard** (covers "recent activity" for any current date).
- Date-range queries map the range to a shard set and read only those.
- Pagination beyond the default window triggers older-shard loads on demand.
- No shard-coordination lock needed: each month's file is append-only and only the current-month shard is actively written.

### Legacy migration

- `ddx agent log reindex` reads the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` once and splits by each entry's `timestamp` field into the appropriate monthly shards under `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/`.
- After migration, the legacy file is renamed `sessions.jsonl.legacy` (not deleted) and never read again by the server. Hand-removal is the user's call.
- Idempotent: re-running the command merges without duplicating (index row dedup by `id`).

### Revised acceptance criteria (supersedes criteria 1, 5, 6, 7 on original description)

1. **Sharded index writer.** Single shared call site (from `service.Execute` or equivalent wrapper) appends one line to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, computing the shard from `time.Now().UTC()`. Test: write across a simulated month boundary and assert two shards exist with the correct split.

5. **Legacy migration shards too.** `ddx agent log reindex` splits `sessions.jsonl` into monthly shards by entry timestamp. Integration test uses a fixture with entries across 3 distinct months and asserts 3 shards are produced with correct contents and no duplicates on re-run.

6. **Server reads the shard directory.** `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` reads `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/*.jsonl`, respects any date-range arg, and loads only the shards that intersect the range. Unit test seeds 6 monthly shards and asserts a 2-month query reads exactly 2 files.

7. **Perf on sharded index.** Default Sessions view (current + previous shard) resolves in ≤50ms p95 on a fixture with 12 monthly shards × 10k entries each (120k total). Capture baseline (single-file read of same corpus) vs sharded in bead notes.

(Criteria 2, 3, 4, 8, 9 from the original acceptance list remain as written.)

### Scope clarifier

This amendment does not change the single-call-site writer guarantee or the lazy-body resolver — those remain the load-bearing ideas. The amendment is specifically about **how the pointer index is physically stored**.

.ddx/executions/20260418T060325-e4e5ad24/manifest.json:13:    "description": "The embedded ddx-agent harness honors the per-run SessionLogDir override (agent_runner.go:136-138: `logDir := opts.SessionLogDir; if empty fall back to r.Config.SessionLogDir`) so that execute-bead runs drop their JSONL trace inside the bundle at .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/embedded/agent-*.jsonl.\n\nThe claude harness does NOT honor that override. claude_stream.go:327 reads only r.Config.SessionLogDir, so every claude execute-bead run writes its trace into the runner-wide default (`.ddx/agent-logs/`) and the bundle's embedded/ dir stays empty. execute_bead.go:446 already passes SessionLogDir: embeddedStateDir through RunOptions; it just isn't used by the claude path.\n\nEmpirical evidence from axon's 2026-04-15 ADR-018 session: 2 runs with `harness: agent` produced 34-51 MB traces in embedded/; 23 runs with `harness: claude` left embedded/ empty while `.ddx/agent-logs/` grew to 162 MB.\n\nFix: plumb opts.SessionLogDir into runClaudeStreaming (either accept it as a parameter or read it off resolvedOpts) and prefer it over r.Config.SessionLogDir when non-empty. Matches the embedded harness behavior exactly.\n\nParity matters: users want per-run forensic traces attached to the bundle regardless of which harness ran the bead.",
.ddx/executions/20260418T060325-e4e5ad24/manifest.json:14:    "acceptance": "- [ ] claude_stream.go:runClaudeStreaming uses opts.SessionLogDir when non-empty, falling back to r.Config.SessionLogDir (matches agent_runner.go:136-138 pattern)\n- [ ] A claude-harness execute-bead run writes agent-\u003csessionID\u003e.jsonl into the bundle's embedded/ dir, not .ddx/agent-logs/\n- [ ] Unit test covers the override precedence for the claude path\n- [ ] Regression test: existing claude runs without execute-bead context still write to DefaultLogDir\n- [ ] Any other harnesses that emit JSONL traces (gemini, codex, etc.) audited and brought to the same override-respecting behavior",
.ddx/executions/20260422T205948-1f58b550/prompt.md:5:## Observed

The Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be "an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model" — which is not what the current implementation does.

## Ground truth (current implementation)

`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —

- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.
- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.
- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.
- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.

## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)

- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: "how is harness/provider/model X doing?" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.
- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).
- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.

## Proposed direction

### Part 1 — Reshape the data source

- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.
- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.
- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.
- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.

### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)

- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.
- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.
- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.

### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)

- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.

### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path

- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.
- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.

## Out of scope

- The `comparisons` / "compare arms" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).
- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).
- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.
.ddx/executions/20260422T205948-1f58b550/prompt.md:8:**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.

**Acceptance criteria:**

1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.

2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.

3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.

4. **First-pass perf targets.**
   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.
   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.
   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.

5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**
   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.

6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.

7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.

8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.

9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.

.ddx/executions/20260422T205940-cc981ebc/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Observed\n\n`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:\n\n- **6,936 named \"001\"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.\n- **344 named \"002\"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.\n- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).\n- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are \"alive\" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.\n- State file size: 1.8 MB.\n\n## Root cause\n\n1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.\n2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.\n3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.\n\n## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)\n\nBoth `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nThree independently-landable fixes:\n\n### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)\n\n- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).\n- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.\n- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.\n\n### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)\n\n- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.\n- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\\w+\\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.\n- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.\n\n### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI\n\n- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-\u003ctimestamp\u003e`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).\n- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.\n- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Moving state to SQLite.\n- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)\n- Renaming \"001\" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.",
.ddx/executions/20260422T205940-cc981ebc/prompt.md:5:## Observed

`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:

- **6,936 named "001"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.
- **344 named "002"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.
- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).
- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are "alive" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.
- State file size: 1.8 MB.

## Root cause

1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.
2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.
3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.

## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)

Both `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.

## Proposed direction

Three independently-landable fixes:

### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)

- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).
- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.
- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.

### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)

- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.
- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\w+\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.
- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.

### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI

- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-<timestamp>`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).
- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.
- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.

## Out of scope

- Moving state to SQLite.
- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)
- Renaming "001" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.

.ddx/executions/20260416T180825-2aaff03d/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Scope\n\nexecute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it\nmaterializes (`.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,\n`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run\nreturns. Successful bead commits reach main via the \"Merge bead\" path, but the\nevidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that\ngap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and\nreturns the working tree clean.\n\nConcrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill\nthat imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` directories\n(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.\nThat backfill must not need to happen again.\n\n## Files to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the\n  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and\n  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and\n  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:\n  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's\n      evidence files, single commit), OR\n  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt-id\u003e]` commit\n      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same\n      merge transaction.\n  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent\n  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must\n  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent\n  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded\n  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After\n  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,\n  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the\n  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/`),\n  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise (\"orchestrator left worktree\n  dirty: \u003cpath\u003e\").\n- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,\n  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and\n  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean\n  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping\n  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new\n  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.\n\n## Files NOT to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead\n  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /\n  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep\n  telling the agent to `git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e` only. The fix belongs in\n  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.\n- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,\n  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit\n  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.\n\n## Cross-Bead Context\n\n- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` (\"Evolve execute-bead into a tracked\n  execution-evidence workflow\", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this\n  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.\n- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md\n  \"Merge Policy\" section, FEAT-006 \"Execution Trail Preservation in\n  Downstream Merges\" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and\n  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md \"Branch Merge Policy\" section.\n- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and\n  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths\n  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick\n  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.\n- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc (\"anchor SessionLogDir to project root\") gives\n  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is\n  the analogous fix for execution evidence.\n\n## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)\n\n\u003e **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]\n\u003e Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git\n\u003e history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports\n\u003e read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`\n\u003e contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from\n\u003e git history alone.\n\u003e\n\u003e **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**\n\u003e - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker\n\u003e   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then\n\u003e   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target\n\u003e   branch HEAD after the merge.\n\u003e - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing\n\u003e   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e\n\u003e   \u003cclosing_commit_sha\u003e` still succeeds on main after the merge.\n\nThis bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the\nper-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely\npreserved across merges.\n\n## Verification Commands\n\n- `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`\n  passes (new regression test, see AC below).\n- `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes\n  (full suite; no regressions).\n- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run\n  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,\n  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show\n  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/` in the HEAD commit\n  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).",

(2) go test ./internal/server/... -run \"TestGraphQLWorkers|TestGraphQLAgentSessions|TestGraphQLExecutions|TestGraphQLPersonas|TestGraphQLCoordinators\" -count=1 passes with all tests green. (5) Opus review of the added tests confirms they would catch a broken resolver (not just test that the resolver exists).",
.ddx/executions/20260415T044256-183ff980/prompt.md:5:Systematic acceptance drift: Stage 2.5 (commits), 2.6 (workers+sessions+executions), and 2.7 (personas+coordinatorMetrics) all landed with real resolver implementations but zero resolver-level integration tests added, despite each bead explicitly listing "integration tests against real <source>" as an acceptance criterion. Opus reviewer flagged this consistently.

Why it was allowed to land: underlying stores (workers_test.go, ddxexec, persona loader, land coordinator) have their own unit tests, so go build and go test ./internal/server/... pass cleanly. The resolver-to-store path is unverified but not broken.

Scope: add end-to-end integration tests (same pattern as graphql_node_test.go and graphql_beads_test.go) that spin up server.New with real state and POST GraphQL queries, for:
- TestGraphQLWorkers (ddxgraphql.Workers + WorkersByProject + Worker(id))
- TestGraphQLAgentSessions (ddxgraphql.AgentSessions + AgentSession(id))
- TestGraphQLExecutions (ddxgraphql.ExecDefinitions + ExecRuns + ExecDefinition(id) + ExecRun(id))
- TestGraphQLPersonas (ddxgraphql.Personas + Persona(id) + PersonaByRole + PersonaLoader wiring via direct call not StateProvider)
- TestGraphQLCoordinators (ddxgraphql.Coordinators + CoordinatorMetricsByProject)
- TestGraphQLCommitsWithMutatingDataset (strengthens the existing TestGraphQLCommits to cover mutation-during-pagination, matching the cursor stability concern)

Each test: real temp server, real stores, real GraphQL POST via httptest, assert Relay connection shape and a representative data item. Tests should match the pattern in cli/internal/server/graphql_node_test.go (TestGraphQLNodeInfo) and graphql_beads_test.go (TestGraphQLBeadsPagination).

File: new cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go or split per-resolver files matching the existing convention.

(3) Each test uses real temp server state, not mocks. (4) The test file(s) follow the existing graphql_node_test.go pattern — POST /graphql via httptest, decode JSON response, assert connection shape and a representative data item. (5) Opus review of the added tests confirms they would catch a broken resolver (not just test that the resolver exists).
.ddx/executions/20260416T044613-0f04e8ce/manifest.json:14:    "description": "## Scope\n\nexecute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it\nmaterializes (`.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,\n`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run\nreturns. Successful bead commits reach main via the \"Merge bead\" path, but the\nevidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that\ngap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and\nreturns the working tree clean.\n\nConcrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill\nthat imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` directories\n(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.\nThat backfill must not need to happen again.\n\n## Files to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the\n  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and\n  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and\n  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:\n  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's\n      evidence files, single commit), OR\n  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [\u003cattempt-id\u003e]` commit\n      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same\n      merge transaction.\n  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent\n  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must\n  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent\n  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded\n  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After\n  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,\n  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the\n  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/`),\n  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise (\"orchestrator left worktree\n  dirty: \u003cpath\u003e\").\n- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,\n  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and\n  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean\n  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping\n  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new\n  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.\n\n## Files NOT to Touch\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead\n  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /\n  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep\n  telling the agent to `git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e` only. The fix belongs in\n  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.\n- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,\n  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit\n  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.\n\n## Cross-Bead Context\n\n- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` (\"Evolve execute-bead into a tracked\n  execution-evidence workflow\", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this\n  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.\n- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md\n  \"Merge Policy\" section, FEAT-006 \"Execution Trail Preservation in\n  Downstream Merges\" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and\n  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md \"Branch Merge Policy\" section.\n- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and\n  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see\n  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths\n  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick\n  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.\n- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc (\"anchor SessionLogDir to project root\") gives\n  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is\n  the analogous fix for execution evidence.\n\n## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)\n\n\u003e **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]\n\u003e Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git\n\u003e history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports\n\u003e read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`\n\u003e contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from\n\u003e git history alone.\n\u003e\n\u003e **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**\n\u003e - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker\n\u003e   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then\n\u003e   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target\n\u003e   branch HEAD after the merge.\n\u003e - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing\n\u003e   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e\n\u003e   \u003cclosing_commit_sha\u003e` still succeeds on main after the merge.\n\nThis bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the\nper-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely\npreserved across merges.\n\n## Verification Commands\n\n- `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`\n  passes (new regression test, see AC below).\n- `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes\n  (full suite; no regressions).\n- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run\n  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,\n  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show\n  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/` in the HEAD commit\n  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).",
.ddx/executions/20260416T044613-0f04e8ce/prompt.md:5:## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.
- `AGENTS.md`, `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md`,
  `skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md` — the policy is already correct as of commit
  3a2a0c5. This bead implements the policy; it does not amend it.

## Cross-Bead Context

- Parent epic `ddx-32b3008e` ("Evolve execute-bead into a tracked
  execution-evidence workflow", P0, spec-id FEAT-006) is the meta for this
  work. This bead is the first execution-layer task under that epic.
- The trail-preservation policy is codified in commit 3a2a0c5: AGENTS.md
  "Merge Policy" section, FEAT-006 "Execution Trail Preservation in
  Downstream Merges" section (with 3 acceptance criteria), and
  skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md "Branch Merge Policy" section.
- The land flow today uses a mix of `git merge --no-ff` and
  `git update-ref` (for bare-repo worktrees) — see
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:166-544 for the three code paths
  that touch the merge. Any of them may be the right insertion point; pick
  the one that runs LAST after the agent commit is finalized.
- The bug-fix commit 2792dfc ("anchor SessionLogDir to project root") gives
  the orchestrator a reliable absolute path for session logs. This bead is
  the analogous fix for execution evidence.

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

## Verification Commands

- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`
  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).

## Spec Excerpt (FEAT-006 Execution Trail Preservation, normative)

> **Execution Trail Preservation in Downstream Merges** [...]
> Bead records store `closing_commit_sha` as a pointer back into git
> history; routing, cost, latency, retry-count, and tier-escalation reports
> read these commits directly. The trail realizes the `output = bead(input)`
> contract the system is built on: every bead attempt is recoverable from
> git history alone.
>
> **Acceptance Criteria (execution trail preservation):**
> - Given a branch containing at least one `chore: update tracker
>   (execute-bead` commit, when the branch is merged to its target, then
>   every commit SHA on the feature branch remains reachable from the target
>   branch HEAD after the merge.
> - Given a bead has `closing_commit_sha` set, when the branch containing
>   that commit is merged to main, then `git cat-file -e
>   <closing_commit_sha>` still succeeds on main after the merge.

This bead adds a fourth, implementation-layer criterion: that the
per-attempt evidence dir is *present in the trail at all*, not merely
preserved across merges.

## Verification Commands

- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent -run TestExecuteBeadLandsEvidence`
  passes (new regression test, see AC below).
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/server ./cmd` passes
  (full suite; no regressions).
- Manual dogfooding: in a throwaway clone, run
  `ddx agent execute-loop --once` against a ready bead. After it returns,
  `git status --porcelain` MUST print nothing. `git log -1 --stat` MUST show
  at least one file under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/` in the HEAD commit
  (or its immediate predecessor in the same merge).
.ddx/executions/20260416T181205-b5419982/prompt.md:5:## Scope

execute-bead's orchestrator currently leaves the per-attempt evidence files it
materializes (`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/manifest.json`, `prompt.md`,
`result.json`, `checks.json`, `usage.json`) untracked after the agent run
returns. Successful bead commits reach main via the "Merge bead" path, but the
evidence sits in the working tree unstaged and orphaned. This bead closes that
gap: after the agent finishes, DDx (not the agent) stages the evidence and
returns the working tree clean.

Concrete evidence of the gap: commit 98e379b on main is a one-shot backfill
that imports 254 historical `.ddx/executions/<ts>-<hash>/` directories
(2026-04-11 → 2026-04-15) that were never staged by the execute-bead pipeline.
That backfill must not need to happen again.

## Files to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go` — the land/merge flow. After the
  agent's commit is produced (or after `Merge()` / bare-ref update), and
  before returning `ExecuteBeadReport`, stage the per-attempt evidence dir and
  fold it into the merge. Two shapes are acceptable:
  (a) amend the evidence into the bead's own commit (agent's commit + DDx's
      evidence files, single commit), OR
  (b) emit a trailing `chore: add execution evidence [<attempt-id>]` commit
      on the working branch as a sibling to the bead's commit, in the same
      merge transaction.
  Choose (b) if amending the agent's commit would rewrite its SHA — the agent
  commit's SHA is referenced by `session_id` / `closing_commit_sha` and must
  not move. Choose (a) only if the evidence can be staged BEFORE the agent
  commits (e.g. the agent is told via `git add` hooks to pick up a DDx-seeded
  index). Pragmatic recommendation: (b).
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` — the per-run orchestrator entry. After
  the executor returns, verify the working tree is clean; if it is not,
  diagnose untracked files introduced by this run, stage them into the
  trailing evidence commit (if they are under `.ddx/executions/<attempt>/`),
  and fail the run with a clear error otherwise ("orchestrator left worktree
  dirty: <path>").
- `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — the server-worker path (`runWorker`,
  `buildAgentRunner`). Same contract applies: after `ExecuteBead` returns and
  `LandBeadResult` is called, the worker must verify the worktree is clean
  before declaring the attempt complete. If the land flow is still dropping
  evidence, surface that as a test failure, not a silent leak.
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go` OR a new
  `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_evidence_test.go` — regression tests.

## Files NOT to Touch

- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go` lines 875-960 — the per-bead
  instructions text (`executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText` /
  `executeBeadInstructionsAgentText`). Those are agent-facing and must keep
  telling the agent to `git add <specific-paths>` only. The fix belongs in
  the orchestrator, not the prompt: DDx wrote the evidence, DDx commits it.

The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Harness, WorkerRecord.Model, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.

This bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.

## Required changes

### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)
Register alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:
  --json         emit raw JSON array
  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C
  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)

### 2. Data sources (two paths)
A. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.
B. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:
   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)
   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, harness, model, started_at

### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan
For each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:
  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)
  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)
  - Read execute-loop-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available
  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields

### 4. Table output (default)
Columns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | HARNESS | MODEL | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS
- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)
- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)
- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID

### 5. Merge and deduplicate
When both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.

## Key files
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()
- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)
- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)
.ddx/executions/20260414T224458-37674b95/manifest.json:31:          "body": "Stage 2.2 (node/projects resolvers, commit 7e4ce6a) established the resolver pattern cleanly but left five decisions open that affect every subsequent resolver bead. Lock these in at Stage 2.3 (beads + beadsByProject) before the pattern propagates to 2.4-2.7.\n\n1. StateProvider interface split\n   Current: single StateProvider in graphql/resolver_node.go with 3 methods (GetNodeSnapshot/GetProjectSnapshots/GetProjectSnapshotByID). Stage 2.3 will add GetBeads/GetBeadByID/GetBeadsByProject — the interface will balloon to 50+ methods across all stages. Recommendation: split into per-resolver-group sub-interfaces (NodeState, BeadState, DocumentState, WorkerState, etc.) each defined next to its resolver file. The top-level Resolver.State field embeds a composite interface that combines them. File: introduce state_provider.go with the composite interface and move the existing StateProvider methods into a new NodeState interface.\n\n2. Stable-key cursors (Relay connections)\n   Current: resolver_node.go uses encodeCursor/decodeCursor that emit base64(\"cursor:\u003cslice-index\u003e\"). Slice-index cursors break under insert/delete between pages — fine for projects (rarely mutate) but broken for beads (mutated constantly). Stage 2.3 MUST use a stable-key cursor: base64 of (sort-key, id) or similar. Do not copy-paste the slice-index helpers from resolver_node.go; introduce stable-key helpers in a shared graphql/cursors.go and use them for beads/documents/commits/sessions.\n\n3. Snapshot struct duplication vs direct domain types\n   Current: graphql/resolver_node.go defines ProjectSnapshot that mirrors server.ProjectEntry field-for-field. Fine for 3 fields. Will become painful if every state type needs a shadow struct (Bead, Document, Worker, Session, ExecutionRun, Persona, Commit, ...). Decide now: either accept the snapshot pattern and live with duplication, OR let the graphql package depend on narrowly-factored state types directly. Pick ONE before Stage 2.3 so we do not end up with both patterns.\n\n4. r.State == nil nil-check\n   Current: resolver_node.go has if r.State == nil { return ...} guards in every method. Will copy-paste into 50 resolvers. Refactor: Resolver constructor rejects nil state, drop the runtime check. Trivial fix (one constructor), huge win in readability across 50 call sites.\n\n5. node(id) prefix dispatch\n   Current: node(id) switches on \"node-\"/\"proj-\" prefixes with an if-else chain. Stage 2.3 adds bead- prefix. Stage 2.4-2.7 add doc-, worker-, session-, exec-, persona- prefixes. The chain grows to 9 branches. Better: prefix-to-loader map (or a registry that each resolver file registers into from its init()). Lock this at 2.3 before the chain grows.\n\nNone of these are defects in 2.2. They are shape decisions that SHOULD be made at 2.3 to avoid technical debt accumulation across 2.4-2.7.",
.ddx/executions/20260414T081337-928daf7a/manifest.json:14:    "description": "The server knows everything about running workers (state, harness, model, bead, attempts, prompt file, structured event log, token/cost/duration) via /api/agent/workers endpoints but the web UI has no live workers view. When an operator selects the eitri node they should land on (or reach in one click) an Agents page that lists every running worker from /api/agent/workers with bead-id, harness, model, state, elapsed, and latest status line. Clicking a row should open a worker detail page showing: (1) the rendered bead prompt from .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/prompt.md, (2) the structured session log stream from .ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-\u003csession\u003e.jsonl rendered via FormatSessionLogLines (so tool calls, turn counts, and timings are readable), (3) a session utilization panel with input/output/total tokens, cost-if-available, duration, attempts and successes/failures from the worker record, and (4) the base-rev and result-rev link once the worker has made progress. Polling at ~2s is fine for v1; no WebSocket required.\\n\\nBacking endpoints that already exist:\\n- GET /api/agent/workers -\u003e list\\n- GET /api/agent/workers/:id -\u003e detail\\n- GET /api/agent/workers/:id/log -\u003e stdout+stderr streams\\n\\nMissing backing endpoints (if not already there): GET /api/agent/workers/:id/prompt to serve prompt.md content without requiring the UI to know the execution bundle path; optionally /api/agent/workers/:id/events to serve the structured JSONL stream if /log doesn't already cover it.",

Playwright test covers: list-shows-running-worker, list-refreshes-on-state-change, detail-renders-prompt-log-and-utilization.",

.ddx/executions/20260414T101322-a7e5a51d/manifest.json:14:    "description": "ddx agent log is fully implemented (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go lines 745-807) and reads .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl. Each SessionEntry has a Correlation map (cli/internal/agent/types.go) that includes bead_id when the session was created by execute-bead. However there is no way to filter sessions by bead — you must know the session ID in advance or scroll through all recent sessions.\n\nThis bead adds --bead \u003cid\u003e filtering and a per-bead attempt summary view to the existing command.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. --bead flag on ddx agent log (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nWhen --bead \u003cbead-id\u003e is provided:\n  - Scan all SessionEntry records in sessions.jsonl\n  - Filter to entries where Correlation[\"bead_id\"] == bead-id\n  - Sort ascending by Timestamp (attempt order)\n  - Display in attempt-history table format (see below)\n\nNo change to the default behavior (no --bead = show recent N sessions as today).\n\n### 2. Attempt history table columns\nATTEMPT | STARTED | DURATION | HARNESS | MODEL | OUTCOME | TOKENS | COST | SESSION\n- ATTEMPT: 1-based index in chronological order\n- OUTCOME: merged / preserved / no-changes / error (from ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome via Correlation or result.json)\n- SESSION: truncated session ID (8 chars) for use with ddx agent log \u003csession-id\u003e\n- COST: formatted as /bin/zsh.0042 or 'local' if cost_usd == 0\n\n### 3. .ddx/executions/20260414T101322-a7e5a51d/prompt.md:5:ddx agent log is fully implemented (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go lines 745-807) and reads .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl. Each SessionEntry has a Correlation map (cli/internal/agent/types.go) that includes bead_id when the session was created by execute-bead. However there is no way to filter sessions by bead — you must know the session ID in advance or scroll through all recent sessions.

This bead adds --bead <id> filtering and a per-bead attempt summary view to the existing command.

## Required changes

### 1. --bead flag on ddx agent log (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)
When --bead <bead-id> is provided:
  - Scan all SessionEntry records in sessions.jsonl
  - Filter to entries where Correlation["bead_id"] == bead-id
  - Sort ascending by Timestamp (attempt order)
  - Display in attempt-history table format (see below)

No change to the default behavior (no --bead = show recent N sessions as today).

### 2. Attempt history table columns
ATTEMPT | STARTED | DURATION | HARNESS | MODEL | OUTCOME | TOKENS | COST | SESSION
- ATTEMPT: 1-based index in chronological order
- OUTCOME: merged / preserved / no-changes / error (from ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome via Correlation or result.json)
- SESSION: truncated session ID (8 chars) for use with ddx agent log <session-id>
- COST: formatted as /bin/zsh.0042 or 'local' if cost_usd == 0

### 3. Outcome resolution
SessionEntry does not directly store outcome — it stores exit_code and error. The full outcome is in .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/result.json (ExecuteBeadResult struct). When rendering the attempt table:
  - Use Correlation["attempt_id"] to find .ddx/executions/{attempt_id}/result.json
  - Read ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome from that file
  - Fall back to exit_code == 0 ? 'success' : 'error' if result.json is absent

### 4. Summary line
After the table, print a one-line summary:
  '<bead-id>: N attempts, M merged, K preserved, J errors. Last attempt: <elapsed> ago.'

### 5. --json passthrough
When --bead and --json are both set, emit the filtered SessionEntry array as JSON with an added outcome field resolved from result.json.

## Key files
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — newAgentLogCommand() lines 745-807, add --bead flag and filter path
- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry struct (lines 94-122), Correlation map field
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadResult struct (lines 30-64), artifact paths convention
- .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl — runtime data source
